title: po CLI: spend aggregator + JSON output on read commands
description:
The po CLI is the tool surface chief agents (and the future nanocorps
dashboard) call to inspect runs. Two gaps block programmatic consumption.

## Gaps

1. **No spend/cost aggregator.** Each run_dir emits Claude usage JSON
   (visible in `po artifacts` output), but there's no command that rolls
   it up by issue, formula, role, or time-window. Chief can't answer
   "how much have we spent today?" without scripting around artifacts.
   Dashboard would need the same data.

2. **No `--json` on read commands.** `po status`, `po list`, `po show`,
   `po sessions` render rich Typer/Rich output. Chief and dashboard
   can't parse rendered terminal output reliably.

## Proposed

- `po spend [--issue-id <id>] [--since <window>] [--by formula|role|day]
  [--json]` — aggregate cost from run_dir usage JSON. Default: last 7d,
  grouped by role. Honor the same `--since` syntax as `po status`
  (relative `1h`/`30m`/`2d` or ISO-8601).
- `--json` flag on `po status`, `po list`, `po show`, `po sessions`.
  Stable shapes; document in `prefect_orchestration/docs/json-schemas.md`.
- `--json` on `po watch` emits NDJSON (one event per line) so
  dashboards can consume the merged Prefect+artifact stream directly.

## Out of scope

- `po tui` audit — handled separately.
- Anything Prefect's UI (:4200) covers well: flow-graph rendering, task
  duration charts, run history search.
- Anything bd covers: bead detail, dep graph, search.
- `po dispatch` shortcuts — would bake formula names; pack-agnostic rule.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `po spend` exists, reads run_dir cost JSON, supports `--issue-id`,
      `--since`, `--by formula|role|day`, `--json`. Default human
      output; `--json` returns shape documented in schemas doc.
- [ ] `--json` flag on `status`, `list`, `show`, `sessions` returns
      stable, documented shapes.
- [ ] `po watch --json` emits NDJSON.
- [ ] Tests: spend math correctness, JSON shape stability, NDJSON
      framing.
- [ ] Schemas documented in `prefect_orchestration/docs/json-schemas.md`.

## Why this matters

Chief becomes self-sufficient on spend reporting; dashboard later reads
the same shapes. No second cost-aggregation layer in nanocorps.
acceptance_criteria: po spend works with --since/--by/--json; --json on status/list/show/sessions; po watch --json NDJSON; schemas documented
notes: Source: 2026-04-29 nanocorps brainstorm session — chief tool-surface audit. Constraint: pack-agnostic; don't duplicate Prefect UI or bd.
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: po retry: detect original formula from prior flow run instead of defaulting to software-dev-full
description:
**Problem.** `po retry <issue-id>` always uses `software-dev-full` as the formula, regardless of what formula the original failed/cancelled run used. This is a hardcoded constant from before `software-dev-fast` existed:

```python
# prefect_orchestration/retry.py:46
DEFAULT_FORMULA = "software-dev-full"

# line 182
def retry(... formula: str = DEFAULT_FORMULA, ...)
```

**Impact.** A fast run that fails (e.g., rate limit, transient error) gets retried as full — different flow shape, different time/cost profile, different role progression. Specifically reproduced today with `prefect-orchestration-prv`:

- Original dispatch: `po run software-dev-fast --issue-id prv ...` → crashed at build with RateLimitError
- Retry: `po retry prefect-orchestration-prv` → silently relaunched as `software_dev_full`, going through triage/baseline/plan-critic loop/etc. — not what the operator asked for.

**Goal.** Retry should preserve the original formula by default. Operator can still override with `--formula <name>` if they explicitly want to switch (e.g., escalate fast → full after a fast attempt failed).

**Implementation options** (pick whichever the planner finds simpler):

**Option A: Read formula from Prefect flow_run history.**
- The original flow_run's `flow_name` is available via `prefect.client.PrefectClient().read_flow_runs(flow_run_filter=FlowRunFilter(name={"like_": "<issue-id>"}), sort=FlowRunSort.START_TIME_DESC, limit=1)`. The most-recent run's `flow.name` is the formula key (`software_dev_fast` or `software_dev_full`).
- Map flow_name → entry-point name (`software_dev_fast` → `software-dev-fast`, `software_dev_full` → `software-dev-full`). Lookup in `entry_points(group="po.formulas")` by callable identity.
- If lookup fails (unknown flow_name, deleted entry point), fall back to `DEFAULT_FORMULA` with a warning printed.

**Option B: Stamp formula name into run_dir at dispatch.**
- `cli.py::run` writes `<run_dir>/.po-formula` containing the formula name on every dispatch.
- `retry.py` reads it; if missing, falls back to `DEFAULT_FORMULA`.
- Simpler than A (no Prefect query), but requires every formula to have a run_dir convention. Most do (`<rig>/.planning/software-dev-full/<issue>/`); confirm before relying on it.

**Recommendation: Option B.** Less coupling to Prefect's API, deterministic, easy to test. Stamp the file at dispatch time, read at retry time. If a future formula doesn't write to a run_dir at all, retry can still fall back gracefully.

**Files.**
- `prefect_orchestration/cli.py::run` — stamp `<run_dir>/.po-formula` (single line: formula name).
- `prefect_orchestration/retry.py::retry` — read `.po-formula` if not explicitly passed; fall through to `DEFAULT_FORMULA` with a clear log line if missing.
- `tests/test_retry.py` — fixture covering: explicit `--formula` overrides; missing `.po-formula` falls back; present `.po-formula` is honored.

**Acceptance.**
- `po run software-dev-fast --issue-id <id> ...` writes `<run_dir>/.po-formula` containing `software-dev-fast`.
- `po retry <id>` (no `--formula` flag) reads that file and relaunches as `software-dev-fast`.
- `po retry <id> --formula software-dev-full` overrides and uses full (operator escalation case).
- Old runs without the stamp file fall back to `DEFAULT_FORMULA` with a single-line warning: `po retry: no .po-formula stamp found, defaulting to software-dev-full`.
- Tests pass.

**Out of scope.**
- Changing `DEFAULT_FORMULA` itself (would break old runs that need the fallback).
- Migrating existing in-progress runs to add the stamp retroactively (operator can pass `--formula` explicitly).

**Reference.**
- prefect-orchestration-prv (today's prv retry that surfaced this bug — got switched from fast to full silently).
notes:
**Scope correction (architectural).** The original issue described falling back to `DEFAULT_FORMULA = "software-dev-full"` when the stamp file is missing. That's wrong — it's a pack/core separation leak. Core (`prefect-orchestration`) does not know which packs the operator has installed. Hardcoding any specific formula name in core means core is taking a stance about which pack is "blessed," which breaks the entry-points abstraction the rest of the design rests on.

**Updated design:** **no default formula**. Retry must derive it.

Resolution order:
1. **Explicit `--formula <name>`** flag wins.
2. **Stamp file** `<run_dir>/.po-formula` (written by `cli.py::run` at dispatch).
3. **Prefect flow_run history** as a fallback for old runs that don't have the stamp file (Option A from the original issue).
4. **None of the above** → exit non-zero with a clear error: `po retry: cannot determine original formula. Pass --formula <name> explicitly. Available: po list`

Specifically: **delete `DEFAULT_FORMULA` entirely**. The `formula: str = DEFAULT_FORMULA` parameter default at retry.py:182 becomes `formula: str | None = None`, and the function resolves the name in priority order above before passing to `_load_formula`.

Why both (2) and (3): the stamp file is forward-looking (cleaner, deterministic, easy to test) but every existing in-flight run lacks it. The Prefect history fallback covers those without forcing the operator to manually pass `--formula` for every legacy retry. Once stamps are universal, Option A becomes a belt-and-suspenders backup that can be removed.

**Updated acceptance**:
- `DEFAULT_FORMULA` constant deleted from `retry.py`.
- `po retry <id>` with no flag and no stamp file and no Prefect history → clean error message + non-zero exit, NOT a silent fallback.
- Stamp file written on every successful dispatch.
- Tests cover all four resolution paths (explicit / stamp / Prefect / error).

**Why this matters**: future packs (e.g. po-formulas-data-science with its own flows like `data-eda`) shouldn't have their retries silently rerouted to a formula from another pack. Today the symptom is fast→full (within one pack); tomorrow it could be data-eda→software-dev-full (cross-pack), which is a much weirder bug.
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: baseline iter 1 for prefect-orchestration-prv
description:
You are the **tester** capturing a baseline for issue `{{issue_id}}` in rig `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` BEFORE any changes.

The rig (`/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration`) is where `bd` and the project venv live, so run tests from there. When `{{pack_path}}` differs from `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` (cross-repo self-dev), the rig's test suite is still the consumer-side baseline — it exercises whatever distribution is currently installed from the pack. Save all output to `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-prv/baseline.txt`. Auto-detect the toolchain:

```bash
cd /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration
{
  echo "=== BASELINE $(date -Iseconds) ==="
  # Layer-aware pytest — baseline is unit-only. e2e/playwright are
  # inherently slow (real subprocesses, real Prefect server, real
  # browser); the flow's run_tests task runs them separately against
  # the post-change state. Including them here adds 2–10 minutes for
  # no extra signal.
  if [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then
    IGNORES=""
    [ -d tests/e2e ]        && IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore=tests/e2e"
    [ -d tests/playwright ] && IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore=tests/playwright"
    uv run python -m pytest tests/ $IGNORES --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -30
  fi
  [ -f package.json ]   && bun test 2>&1 | tail -30 || true
  [ -f Makefile ]       && make test 2>&1 | tail -30 || true
  [ -f pyproject.toml ] && {
    MAIN_PKG=$(grep -E '^name' pyproject.toml | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*"([^"]+)".*/\1/' | tr - _)
    uv run python -c "import $MAIN_PKG" 2>&1 || echo "import check failed"
  }
  [ -f package.json ]   && bun run build 2>&1 | tail -15 || true
} > /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-prv/baseline.txt 2>&1
```

If the baseline itself is already broken, note it in `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-prv/baseline-notes.md` and continue. Your job is to not make it worse.

Reply with one line: `baseline captured` or `baseline captured with existing failures`.

1. Classify routing flags (downstream roles read these to decide what to skip / run)
2. Pick a **complexity tier** (controls how much pipeline runs)
3. (Optional) for `trivial` issues: do the work yourself + close + return

# Routing flags

- `has_ui` — does this change UI? (gates demo-video step)
- `has_backend` — does this change backend?
- `needs_migration` — schema/DB change?
- `is_docs_only` — docs/comments only, no code? (short-circuits to docs+learn only)

# Complexity tiers (PICK ONE)

| Tier | What runs | Wall-clock | When to pick |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`trivial`** | triager only — you do the work + close, no plan/build/review/test | ~2 min | Typo fix, rename a variable, bump a constant, fix a comment, single-line config tweak, stale-doc cleanup. The change is so obvious + isolated that planning would be ceremony. |
| **`simple`** | triage → plan → build → lint → close (no critics, no tests, no review) | ~10 min | Small bug fix, tiny refactor, single-file feature where the build will be obviously right. Plan is short; lint catches mechanics; no need for a critic to second-guess. |
| **`moderate`** | + plan-critic + build-critic + unit tests + docs + learn (no e2e/playwright, no regression-gate, no live verifier, no ralph, no full-test-gate) | ~20-30 min | Small-to-medium feature, multi-file change with tests, anything where a code reviewer would want to look at the diff. Critics catch real mistakes; unit tests cover the new code. |
| **`complex`** | + e2e/playwright (if has_ui) + regression-gate + deploy-smoke + verifier + demo-video. Ralph + full-test-gate are OPT-IN flags, off by default. | ~30-40 min | Production-impacting change: API contract change, schema/DB migration, security-sensitive code path, breaking change to a published interface, multi-package refactor crossing repo boundaries. |

**Bias toward the lighter tier when uncertain.** The pipeline is expensive — `complex` costs ~30-40 min of API time vs `moderate`'s ~20-25 min vs `simple`'s ~10 min. Picking `trivial` for something that wasn't is the only failure mode that ships unreviewed code; for everything else, `simple` or `moderate` still go through plan + build + lint, and `moderate` adds critics + tests. **You almost never need `complex`** — reserve it for changes whose blast radius is "this could break production for users." Documentation, internal tooling, refactors-with-tests, new features behind a feature flag — all `moderate` or below.

# Trivial path (do-the-work-yourself)

If you picked `trivial`:

1. Make the edit yourself. Use Edit tool to change the file(s).
2. `cd /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` then `git add <files>` + `git commit -m "[prefect-orchestration-prv] <one-line>"`.
3. Verify the change makes sense (re-read the diff with `git -C /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration diff HEAD~1`).
4. Skip the regular triage.json verdict file — just write `triage.json` with `complexity=trivial` flag set so the flow body knows you handled it.

If at any point you realize this isn't actually trivial (the change is bigger than expected, or you can't make the edit cleanly), STOP, set `complexity=simple` or `moderate`, and let the regular pipeline handle it.

# Write the triage summary

Write a one-paragraph summary + flags to `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-prv/triage.md`:

```

The flow body reads this to decide what runs. Write JSON booleans (`true`/`false`, lowercase) — not strings, not Python `True`/`False`:

```bash

```

# Done — close your bead

```bash

```

# Layer definitions (non-overlapping)

- **unit** — individual functions / classes in isolation. Mocking external services (HTTP, DB, subprocess) is fine. No real network, no real subprocesses, no Prefect server. Lives at top of `tests/` (or `tests/unit/` when present).
- **e2e** — integration across **real** dependencies. No mocking of the things under integration. Slower (seconds to minutes per test). Lives at `tests/e2e/`.
- **playwright** — browser-driven UI tests. Skip when there's no UI. Lives at `tests/playwright/`.

If you're writing a test that mocks subprocess calls, it's a unit test. If it spawns the real binary or hits a real Prefect server, it's e2e. Don't put both kinds in the same file.

# Read first

The orchestrator pre-loads the plan + build diff into your prompt below — don't re-`cat` them. Each `cat` is a Claude round-trip (~5-8s opus, ~3s sonnet); skipping the obvious ones saves ~30s per role.

## Plan (pre-loaded)

```
# Plan: `po trace` — CLI to inspect agent traces (qhg)

## Issue Summary

Add a `po trace <issue-id>` subcommand that surfaces Claude Code session data (turns, tool calls, token usage, wall time) from the JSONL files Claude Code writes under `~/.claude/projects/`. Currently getting this data requires a 3-step manual process: `po sessions` → find JSONL → write ad-hoc jq. The goal is a single command usable by both humans diagnosing slow runs and by agent self-improvement workflows.

## Research Summary

**JSONL schema (verified by inspection of live files):**
- File location: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<uuid>.jsonl` where slug = rig_path with all `/` replaced by `-` (e.g. `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` → `-home-ryan-24-Desktop-Code-personal-prefect-orchestration`)
- Record types present: `assistant`, `user`, `attachment`, `queue-operation`, `ai-title`, `last-prompt`
- `assistant` records: `{type, timestamp, message: {role, model?, content: [{type: thinking|tool_use|text, ...}], usage: {input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, ...}}}`
- `user` records: `{type, timestamp, message: {role, content: [{type: tool_result, tool_use_id, is_error, content}, ...]}}` — these are tool results from Claude Code
- `tool_use` content block: `{type: tool_use, name: <ToolName>, input: {...}}`
- `thinking` content block: `{type: thinking, thinking: <str>}`

**Session UUID → role mapping**: Already resolved by `sessions.load_role_sessions()` + `run_lookup.resolve_run_dir()`. The `po sessions` command does the exact lookup needed; `po trace` reuses the same pattern.

**Model field**: The `message` dict on assistant records includes a `model` key (standard Anthropic API response shape). Fallback to `"unknown"` if absent.

**Wall time per turn**: Delta between consecutive `assistant` record timestamps. Overall wall = last_assistant_ts − first_assistant_ts.

**Existing patterns**: `sessions.py`, `artifacts.py`, `run_lookup.py` all follow the same "resolve_run_dir → iterate" pattern. `cli.py` adds a `@app.command()` that delegates to a helper module. Tests use `typer.testing.CliRunner` + `monkeypatch` on `run_lookup.resolve_run_dir`.

## Success Criteria

From the issue:
1. `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh` prints the per-role summary table (ROLE / MODEL / TURNS / TOOLS / IN_TOK / OUT_TOK / CACHE_R / THINK / WALL columns).
2. `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh --role planner` dumps the planner's full transcript (tool calls + args + thinking, per turn).
3. `--json` outputs a jq-pipeable JSON array: `[{role, turn, timestamp, type, content_preview, tokens}, ...]`.
4. Works on in-flight runs (JSONL appended live — reader just reads what's there).
5. Works for any formula using `agent_step` (session UUIDs stored the same way for all).
6. `po artifacts <id>` footer line: `"for full trace: po trace <id>"`.

Other flags from the issue spec: `--tools` (chronological tool-call timeline), `--tokens` (token + cache breakdown table), `--turn N` (single turn detail), `--slow` (turns > N seconds).

## Files to Modify/Create

1. **`/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/prefect_orchestration/trace.py`** (new)  
   Parser + formatters. Keeps all JSONL-reading logic out of cli.py.

2. **`/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/prefect_orchestration/cli.py`** (modify)  
   Add `import trace as _trace`, add `@app.command() def trace(...)`, add footer line in `artifacts` command.

3. **`/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/tests/test_trace.py`** (new)  
   Unit tests with fixture JSONL.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1 — `trace.py`: project slug + JSONL lookup

```python
def _project_slug(rig_path: Path) -> str:
    """Convert abs path to Claude project slug: replace / with -."""
    return str(rig_path).replace("/", "-")

def find_jsonl(uuid: str, rig_path: Path) -> Path | None:
    """Locate <uuid>.jsonl under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/. Fallback: scan all slugs."""
    projects_dir = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects"
    candidate = projects_dir / _project_slug(rig_path) / f"{uuid}.jsonl"
    if candidate.exists():
        return candidate
    # Fallback: scan all project subdirs (handles cross-rig sessions)
    for f in projects_dir.glob(f"*/{uuid}.jsonl"):
        return f
    return None
```

### Step 2 — `trace.py`: JSONL parser

```python
@dataclass
class TurnRecord:
    index: int          # 1-based
    timestamp: str      # ISO-8601
    thinking: bool
    tool_names: list[str]
    tool_inputs_preview: list[str]  # first 80 chars of input repr
    text_preview: str   # first 120 chars of any text block
    in_tok: int
    out_tok: int
    cache_r: int
    cache_w: int
    model: str
    wall_s: float       # seconds since first turn

@dataclass
class RoleTrace:
    role: str
    uuid: str
    turns: list[TurnRecord]
    jsonl_path: Path | None  # None if file not found

def parse_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[TurnRecord]:
    """Read a JSONL file, return one TurnRecord per assistant message."""
    ...
```

Parsing logic:
- Skip non-`assistant` records
- Accumulate usage fields from `message.usage`
- Extract `model` from `message.get("model", "unknown")`
- Per turn: `thinking = any(c["type"]=="thinking" for c in content)`, `tool_names = [c["name"] for c in content if c["type"]=="tool_use"]`
- `wall_s` = `(parse_ts(rec["timestamp"]) - parse_ts(turns[0].timestamp)).total_seconds()`

### Step 3 — `trace.py`: summary stats + formatters

**`summarize(traces: list[RoleTrace]) -> list[RoleSummary]`**:
- Aggregate per role: total turns, total tools, sum tokens, wall = last_ts − first_ts

```python
@dataclass
class RoleSummary:
    role: str
    model: str
    turns: int
    tools: int
    in_tok: int
    out_tok: int
    cache_r: int
    think_turns: int
    wall_s: float
```

**`format_summary_table(summaries)`** → aligned text table (ROLE MODEL TURNS TOOLS IN_TOK OUT_TOK CACHE_R THINK WALL)

**`format_transcript(traces, role)`** → per-turn text block:
```
[Turn 1  T+0:00  planner]  thinking + 2 tools
  Tool: Bash
    $ bd show prefect-orchestration-qhg
  Tool: Read
    /home/.../CLAUDE.md
[Turn 2  T+0:08  planner]  ...
```

**`format_tools_timeline(traces)`** → `[T+M:SS] role: ToolName(preview)`

**`format_tokens_table(summaries)`** → adds CACHE_W column, same shape as summary minus THINK/WALL

**`format_turn_detail(traces, role, turn_n)`** → full turn: thinking text + tool inputs + text blocks

**`format_slow_turns(traces, min_secs)`** → filter turns where `wall_s - prev_wall_s > min_secs`

**`to_json_list(traces)`** → `list[dict]` one entry per (role, turn), suitable for `json.dumps`

### Step 4 — `cli.py`: `po trace` command

```python
@app.command()
def trace(
    issue_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Beads issue id"),
    role: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--role", help="Full transcript for one role"),
    tools: bool = typer.Option(False, "--tools", help="Chronological tool-call timeline"),
    tokens: bool = typer.Option(False, "--tokens", help="Token + cache breakdown table"),
    turn: int | None = typer.Option(None, "--turn", help="Show one specific turn"),
    slow: float | None = typer.Option(None, "--slow", help="Show turns slower than N seconds (default 10 if bare flag)"),
    output_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json", help="Raw JSON output for piping"),
) -> None:
    """Inspect agent traces for an issue's PO run."""
```

1. `loc = _run_lookup.resolve_run_dir(issue_id)`

5. Parse + dispatch to formatter based on flags

Flag priority: `--json` > `--turn` > `--tools` > `--tokens` > `--role` > default summary.

In `artifacts()`, after `typer.echo(_artifacts.render(sections))`, add:

### Step 6 — `tests/test_trace.py`

Fixture: `_make_jsonl(tmp_path, records)` writes a valid JSONL. Fixture `sample_jsonl` creates 3 assistant turns (one with thinking, two with tool_use) with known timestamps and usage fields.

## Verification Strategy

| Criterion | Verification Method | Concrete Check |
|---|---|---|
| Default summary table | Manual smoke + unit test | `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh` → table with ROLE/MODEL/TURNS headers; `test_format_summary_table_headers` asserts all 9 column headers |
| `--role` transcript | Manual smoke | `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh --role planner` → per-turn blocks with tool names visible |
| `--json` output | Unit test | `test_to_json_list_structure`: each dict has `role`, `turn`, `timestamp`, `type`, `tokens` keys; parseable with `json.loads` |
| In-flight runs work | Manual | run `po trace <active-issue>` while a flow is running; no crash, shows partial data |
| `artifacts` footer | Unit test | invoke `po artifacts` in test; assert `"po trace"` in stdout |

## Test Plan

- Layer: **unit** (no subprocess, no real bd/Prefect) → `tests/test_trace.py`
- Fixture JSONL constructed with explicit dicts, not read from live files
- `run_lookup.resolve_run_dir` monkeypatched, `sessions.load_role_sessions` monkeypatched, `_trace.find_jsonl` monkeypatched where needed
- No e2e tests for this issue (too heavyweight for JSONL file parsing)

## Risks

- **`message.model` field availability**: standard Anthropic API response has `model` at `message.model`. If Claude Code omits it in JSONL, fallback to `"unknown"` prevents breakage. Builder should verify on a live JSONL.
- **JSONL not found**: user may invoke `po trace` on an issue that ran before `po sessions` metadata existed, or on a machine that doesn't have `~/.claude/projects/`. Display a per-role warning line (`  <role>: JSONL not found (uuid=<uuid>)`) and continue — never abort the whole command.
- **Large JSONL files**: some runs accumulate thousands of turns. Parser streams line-by-line (no `readlines()` into RAM), so memory stays O(1) per turn. Formatter writes chunks to a list and joins once.

+    if turn is not None:

+    if tools:

+    if tokens:

+        return
+
+    if role is not None:
+        typer.echo(_trace.format_transcript(traces, role))
+        return
+
+    summaries = _trace.summarize(traces)
+    typer.echo(_trace.format_summary_table(summaries))

description:
You are the **builder** implementing the plan for issue `prefect-orchestration-qhg` (build iter 1).

# Read first

The orchestrator pre-loads the plan + triage into your prompt below — you do **not** need to `cat` them. Each `cat`/`bd show` is one Claude round-trip (~5-8s opus, ~3s sonnet); skipping the obvious ones adds up.

```
# Plan: `po trace` — CLI to inspect agent traces (qhg)

## Issue Summary

Add a `po trace <issue-id>` subcommand that surfaces Claude Code session data (turns, tool calls, token usage, wall time) from the JSONL files Claude Code writes under `~/.claude/projects/`. Currently getting this data requires a 3-step manual process: `po sessions` → find JSONL → write ad-hoc jq. The goal is a single command usable by both humans diagnosing slow runs and by agent self-improvement workflows.

## Research Summary

**JSONL schema (verified by inspection of live files):**
- File location: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<uuid>.jsonl` where slug = rig_path with all `/` replaced by `-` (e.g. `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` → `-home-ryan-24-Desktop-Code-personal-prefect-orchestration`)
- Record types present: `assistant`, `user`, `attachment`, `queue-operation`, `ai-title`, `last-prompt`
- `assistant` records: `{type, timestamp, message: {role, model?, content: [{type: thinking|tool_use|text, ...}], usage: {input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, ...}}}`
- `user` records: `{type, timestamp, message: {role, content: [{type: tool_result, tool_use_id, is_error, content}, ...]}}` — these are tool results from Claude Code
- `tool_use` content block: `{type: tool_use, name: <ToolName>, input: {...}}`
- `thinking` content block: `{type: thinking, thinking: <str>}`

**Session UUID → role mapping**: Already resolved by `sessions.load_role_sessions()` + `run_lookup.resolve_run_dir()`. The `po sessions` command does the exact lookup needed; `po trace` reuses the same pattern.

**Model field**: The `message` dict on assistant records includes a `model` key (standard Anthropic API response shape). Fallback to `"unknown"` if absent.

**Wall time per turn**: Delta between consecutive `assistant` record timestamps. Overall wall = last_assistant_ts − first_assistant_ts.

**Existing patterns**: `sessions.py`, `artifacts.py`, `run_lookup.py` all follow the same "resolve_run_dir → iterate" pattern. `cli.py` adds a `@app.command()` that delegates to a helper module. Tests use `typer.testing.CliRunner` + `monkeypatch` on `run_lookup.resolve_run_dir`.

## Success Criteria

From the issue:
1. `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh` prints the per-role summary table (ROLE / MODEL / TURNS / TOOLS / IN_TOK / OUT_TOK / CACHE_R / THINK / WALL columns).
2. `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh --role planner` dumps the planner's full transcript (tool calls + args + thinking, per turn).
3. `--json` outputs a jq-pipeable JSON array: `[{role, turn, timestamp, type, content_preview, tokens}, ...]`.
4. Works on in-flight runs (JSONL appended live — reader just reads what's there).
5. Works for any formula using `agent_step` (session UUIDs stored the same way for all).
6. `po artifacts <id>` footer line: `"for full trace: po trace <id>"`.

Other flags from the issue spec: `--tools` (chronological tool-call timeline), `--tokens` (token + cache breakdown table), `--turn N` (single turn detail), `--slow` (turns > N seconds).

   Parser + formatters. Keeps all JSONL-reading logic out of cli.py.

2. **`/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/prefect_orchestration/cli.py`** (modify)

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1 — `trace.py`: project slug + JSONL lookup

```python
def _project_slug(rig_path: Path) -> str:
    """Convert abs path to Claude project slug: replace / with -."""
    return str(rig_path).replace("/", "-")

def find_jsonl(uuid: str, rig_path: Path) -> Path | None:
    """Locate <uuid>.jsonl under ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/. Fallback: scan all slugs."""
    projects_dir = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects"
    candidate = projects_dir / _project_slug(rig_path) / f"{uuid}.jsonl"
    if candidate.exists():
        return candidate
    # Fallback: scan all project subdirs (handles cross-rig sessions)
    for f in projects_dir.glob(f"*/{uuid}.jsonl"):
        return f
    return None
```

### Step 2 — `trace.py`: JSONL parser

```python
@dataclass
class TurnRecord:
    index: int          # 1-based
    timestamp: str      # ISO-8601
    thinking: bool
    tool_names: list[str]
    tool_inputs_preview: list[str]  # first 80 chars of input repr
    text_preview: str   # first 120 chars of any text block
    in_tok: int
    out_tok: int
    cache_r: int
    cache_w: int
    model: str
    wall_s: float       # seconds since first turn

@dataclass
class RoleTrace:
    role: str
    uuid: str
    turns: list[TurnRecord]
    jsonl_path: Path | None  # None if file not found

def parse_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[TurnRecord]:
    """Read a JSONL file, return one TurnRecord per assistant message."""
    ...
```

Parsing logic:
- Skip non-`assistant` records
- Accumulate usage fields from `message.usage`
- Extract `model` from `message.get("model", "unknown")`
- Per turn: `thinking = any(c["type"]=="thinking" for c in content)`, `tool_names = [c["name"] for c in content if c["type"]=="tool_use"]`
- `wall_s` = `(parse_ts(rec["timestamp"]) - parse_ts(turns[0].timestamp)).total_seconds()`

### Step 3 — `trace.py`: summary stats + formatters

**`summarize(traces: list[RoleTrace]) -> list[RoleSummary]`**:
- Aggregate per role: total turns, total tools, sum tokens, wall = last_ts − first_ts

```python
@dataclass
class RoleSummary:
    role: str
    model: str
    turns: int
    tools: int
    in_tok: int
    out_tok: int
    cache_r: int
    think_turns: int
    wall_s: float
```

**`format_summary_table(summaries)`** → aligned text table (ROLE MODEL TURNS TOOLS IN_TOK OUT_TOK CACHE_R THINK WALL)

**`format_transcript(traces, role)`** → per-turn text block:
```
[Turn 1  T+0:00  planner]  thinking + 2 tools
  Tool: Bash
    $ bd show prefect-orchestration-qhg
  Tool: Read
    /home/.../CLAUDE.md
[Turn 2  T+0:08  planner]  ...
```

**`format_tools_timeline(traces)`** → `[T+M:SS] role: ToolName(preview)`

**`format_tokens_table(summaries)`** → adds CACHE_W column, same shape as summary minus THINK/WALL

**`format_turn_detail(traces, role, turn_n)`** → full turn: thinking text + tool inputs + text blocks

**`format_slow_turns(traces, min_secs)`** → filter turns where `wall_s - prev_wall_s > min_secs`

**`to_json_list(traces)`** → `list[dict]` one entry per (role, turn), suitable for `json.dumps`

### Step 4 — `cli.py`: `po trace` command

```python
@app.command()
def trace(
    issue_id: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Beads issue id"),
    role: str | None = typer.Option(None, "--role", help="Full transcript for one role"),
    tools: bool = typer.Option(False, "--tools", help="Chronological tool-call timeline"),
    tokens: bool = typer.Option(False, "--tokens", help="Token + cache breakdown table"),
    turn: int | None = typer.Option(None, "--turn", help="Show one specific turn"),
    slow: float | None = typer.Option(None, "--slow", help="Show turns slower than N seconds (default 10 if bare flag)"),
    output_json: bool = typer.Option(False, "--json", help="Raw JSON output for piping"),
) -> None:
    """Inspect agent traces for an issue's PO run."""
```

Lookup flow (mirrors `po sessions`):
1. `loc = _run_lookup.resolve_run_dir(issue_id)`
2. Resolve seed bead (copy the seed-bead block from `sessions` command)
3. `metadata = _sessions.load_role_sessions(loc.run_dir, seed_id=..., seed_run_dir=..., rig_path=...)`
4. For each `session_<role>: uuid`, call `_trace.find_jsonl(uuid, loc.rig_path)`
5. Parse + dispatch to formatter based on flags

Flag priority: `--json` > `--turn` > `--tools` > `--tokens` > `--role` > default summary.

### Step 5 — `cli.py`: artifacts footer

In `artifacts()`, after `typer.echo(_artifacts.render(sections))`, add:

```

Fixture: `_make_jsonl(tmp_path, records)` writes a valid JSONL. Fixture `sample_jsonl` creates 3 assistant turns (one with thinking, two with tool_use) with known timestamps and usage fields.

- `test_format_summary_table_headers` — all expected headers present
- `test_format_tools_timeline` — chronological order, contains tool names
- `test_to_json_list_structure` — list of dicts with `role`, `turn`, `timestamp`, `type` keys
- `test_find_jsonl_found` (monkeypatched home) — finds file by uuid
- `test_find_jsonl_not_found` — returns None gracefully
- `test_trace_command_summary` (CliRunner, monkeypatched run_lookup + sessions + trace) — exit 0, table headers in output
- `test_trace_command_no_jsonl` — exits non-zero with clear error when JSONL missing

## Verification Strategy

| Criterion | Verification Method | Concrete Check |
|---|---|---|
| Default summary table | Manual smoke + unit test | `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh` → table with ROLE/MODEL/TURNS headers; `test_format_summary_table_headers` asserts all 9 column headers |
| `--role` transcript | Manual smoke | `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh --role planner` → per-turn blocks with tool names visible |
| `--json` output | Unit test | `test_to_json_list_structure`: each dict has `role`, `turn`, `timestamp`, `type`, `tokens` keys; parseable with `json.loads` |
| In-flight runs work | Manual | run `po trace <active-issue>` while a flow is running; no crash, shows partial data |
| `artifacts` footer | Unit test | invoke `po artifacts` in test; assert `"po trace"` in stdout |

## Test Plan

- Layer: **unit** (no subprocess, no real bd/Prefect) → `tests/test_trace.py`
- Fixture JSONL constructed with explicit dicts, not read from live files
- `run_lookup.resolve_run_dir` monkeypatched, `sessions.load_role_sessions` monkeypatched, `_trace.find_jsonl` monkeypatched where needed
- No e2e tests for this issue (too heavyweight for JSONL file parsing)

## Risks

- **`message.model` field availability**: standard Anthropic API response has `model` at `message.model`. If Claude Code omits it in JSONL, fallback to `"unknown"` prevents breakage. Builder should verify on a live JSONL.
- **JSONL not found**: user may invoke `po trace` on an issue that ran before `po sessions` metadata existed, or on a machine that doesn't have `~/.claude/projects/`. Display a per-role warning line (`  <role>: JSONL not found (uuid=<uuid>)`) and continue — never abort the whole command.
- **Large JSONL files**: some runs accumulate thousands of turns. Parser streams line-by-line (no `readlines()` into RAM), so memory stays O(1) per turn. Formatter writes chunks to a list and joins once.

## Batching tool calls

When you make multiple INDEPENDENT tool calls (e.g., 3 file reads with no dependency between them), put them in a single response — Claude Code runs them in parallel. Sequential calls add 5-8s/turn × N. Batch unless one call's output literally feeds the next.

When `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` == `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` they're the same directory.

# File reservations (concurrent-worker hygiene)

The pack uses `mcp-agent-mail` reservations to prevent same-file collisions across PO workers running concurrent issues:

1. At role start, ensure the project + register an agent name `"prefect-orchestration-qhg-builder"`:
   - `mcp-agent-mail ensure_project project_path="$PWD"` → note `project_key`
   - `mcp-agent-mail register_agent project_key=<above> name="prefect-orchestration-qhg-builder" program="claude-code" model="opus"` (re-registration is idempotent)
2. Reserve the plan's "Affected files" set: `mcp-agent-mail file_reservation_paths paths=[…] agent_name="prefect-orchestration-qhg-builder"` — default 5-min TTL.
3. Denied? Mail the holder via `mcp-agent-mail send_message`, back off 30–60s, retry up to 3×. Do NOT force.
4. After every `git commit`, call `release_file_reservations`. Long builds: `renew_file_reservations` mid-turn.

# Implementation

**Match the implementation's footprint to the actual change.** Read the plan and the affected files. If the plan calls for a 1-line change, your diff should be ~1 line plus a test — NOT a refactor of surrounding code, NOT a new helper, NOT a config schema for hypothetical futures. The plan-critic and build-critic both flag scope creep as a rejection.

Concrete rules:
- **A registry of one entry is a constant.** Don't introduce a registry pattern, plugin loader, or config file for a single value. Three similar lines beats a premature abstraction.
- **A bug fix doesn't drag along cleanup.** If you notice unrelated issues, file a follow-up bead — don't bundle.
- **Don't add error handling, fallbacks, or validation for scenarios that can't happen.** Trust internal callers; only validate at system boundaries.
- **Don't write multi-paragraph docstrings** explaining what well-named code already shows. One short line max for non-obvious WHY.
- **Don't refactor unrelated code** in passing. The diff should be reviewable against the issue alone.

If the plan itself looks padded for the actual scope, push back: write a one-paragraph note in `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-qhg/decision-log.md` explaining what you trimmed and why, and implement the minimal correct version. The build-critic will judge whether your trim was right.

Read the plan and implement it. Commit logical chunks with messages tying back to `prefect-orchestration-qhg`.

## Avoiding cross-worker stomps
- **Prefer `git add <path>` for files you touched** (the plan's Affected Files list + new files you created). Avoid `git add -A` / `git add .` — sweeps up other workers' in-flight work.
- After commit, run `git status --short`. Unrecognized modified/untracked files = another worker's. Leave them; mail the assignee if relevant.
- If another worker's commit arrived mid-build and your changes won't apply cleanly, do NOT `git reset --hard`. Stage your own files only and commit; leave their work alone.

## Decision log (mandatory)
For every non-obvious choice (library pick, architectural pattern, data-model shape, error-handling approach), append to `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-qhg/decision-log.md`:

```markdown
- **Decision**: Used X instead of Y
  **Why**: Z (plan section, CLAUDE.md convention, or technical constraint)
  **Alternatives considered**: <what else you ruled out>
```

The build-critic audits this log; decisions without rationale are review findings.

## Avoid
- Mocks/stubs/placeholder data in production code (the build-critic's anti-mock checklist will block on this — see below)
- `# TODO: replace with real …`, `return {"status": "ok"}` without doing the work, lorem ipsum, fake user IDs
- Feature flags defaulting to mock mode (`USE_REAL_API = False`)
- Print/log instead of actual side effects (`print(f"Would send email to {user}")`)
- `git reset --hard`, `git push --force`, `--no-verify` unless the user explicitly approved
- Premature abstractions for hypothetical future requirements; three similar lines is better than a wrong abstraction

## Test discipline
- New code paths: write the unit test alongside the implementation (don't punt to a separate test phase).
- Existing tests broken by your change: fix them (don't `@skip`/`xfail` to make CI green).
- Don't add `assert mock.called_once()`-style tests that prove nothing about real behaviour.

# Save the diff

When done, persist the diff for the critic:

```bash
git -C /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration diff > "/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-qhg/build-iter-1.diff"

title: po tui v2: tabbed right pane, action controls, stuck detection, width fixes
description:
**Goal.** Redesign po tui anchored on lurking-as-primary, many concurrent runs, action controls, and traceability. Multi-pane stays. Right column collapses from 3 stacked panes (RoleTimeline + TmuxTail + BdShow) into 1 tabbed pane with 4 tabs. The TRACE tab is a separate follow-up issue (depends on the upcoming `po trace` CLI in prefect-orchestration-qhg) — this issue ships LIVE / BD / ACTIONS, leaves a `TRACE: (coming soon)` placeholder.

## Layout

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┐
│ po · 12 runs · 3 running, 1 stuck, 8 done    /:filter│ DETAIL: rig-5vh           │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ [LIVE] [TRACE] [BD] [ACTIONS]│
│ ▶ 5vh  fast      4m   build ⟲1     Add overview  ├─────────────────────────────┤
│   ld9  full     52m   plan  ⟲2  ⚠  IssueList rows│ ━━ LIVE ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━│
│   1qn  full     48m   review ⟲3   RoleTL+tmux fix│ planner✓ → builder⟳ → lint· │
│   3ti  full    1h7m   build ⟲1                   │                              │
│ ─                                                 │ tmux tail (last 30 lines):  │
│   oiu  fast      ✓5m  graph_run fix               │   [live agent output]       │
│   5vh  fast      ✓9m  FlowOverview                │                              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘
[↑↓]sibs [t]tab [→]drill [c]cancel [r]retry [d]dispatch [/]filter [q]quit
```

## Left pane — IssueList changes

1. **Use full panel width.** Fixes the existing bug where rows render at ~25% of available width with empty space to the right (see also: prefect-orchestration-ld9). Inspect `tui/src/components/IssueList.tsx` for fixed-width caps or non-flexed inner Boxes.
2. **Per-row content**: `<id-prefix>  <flow-mode>  <wall>   <step+iter>  <stuck-indicator>  <title-truncated>`.
   - `flow-mode`: `fast` / `full` (single word, gray)
   - `wall`: `4m` / `52m` / `1h7m` (humanized; `>1h` flagged with red color)
   - `step+iter`: current role + iteration glyph, e.g. `build ⟲1`, `review ⟲3`. Source from inner task_run name, not flow_run iteration name (this overlaps with prefect-orchestration-1qn — coordinate; if 1qn lands first this issue piggybacks on its store change).
   - `stuck-indicator`: `⚠` if `wall > 2× tier-typical for current step`. Tier-typical wall is hardcoded heuristics (planner: 5min trivial / 15min complex; builder: 5min trivial / 30min complex; etc.). Conservative — false positives are fine, missed stuck runs are not.
   - `title`: bd issue title (already in `IssueRow.title`), truncated to remaining width.
3. **Group by status**: running on top → stuck flagged in red → done collapsed below an `─` separator (count in header).
4. **Header counts**: `po · N runs · X running, Y stuck, Z done`. Replaces dedicated dashboard pane.
5. **Toggle keybind `[D] show-done`** to expand/collapse done runs (default: collapsed if >5 done).

## Right pane — Tabbed detail

Tabs cycled via `[t]`, or jumped to: `[1]` LIVE, `[2]` TRACE, `[3]` BD, `[4]` ACTIONS.

| Tab | Content | Source |
|---|---|---|
| **LIVE** | RoleTimeline (existing) + TmuxTail (existing, fix session lookup per 1qn) | tmux + Prefect |
| **TRACE** | placeholder this issue: `(coming soon — see prefect-orchestration-qhg)`. Real impl in follow-up. | — |
| **BD** | BdShow output (existing component) | `bd show <id>` |
| **ACTIONS** | List of buttons: Cancel, Retry, Dispatch (opens form for new issue id + flow), Open in Prefect UI (URL), Show artifacts dir | shells `po cancel`/`po retry`/`po run`/`xdg-open` |

LIVE is the **default tab on selection** so first-glance lurking still works without hitting `[t]`.

## Action controls (new keybinds)

| Key | Action | Confirm? |
|---|---|---|
| `c` | Cancel selected run (Prefect flow-run cancel) | Yes (y/n overlay) |
| `r` | Retry selected run (`po retry <id>`) | Yes |
| `d` | Dispatch new run (opens form: issue ID + flow dropdown + rig/rig-path) | No (form itself is the confirmation) |
| `→` | Drill into TRACE tab for selected role-step | No |
| `t` | Cycle tabs | No |
| `1`/`2`/`3`/`4` | Jump to specific tab | No |

The Cancel + Retry confirmations should be modal one-keystroke overlays, not a full form. `c` → "Cancel rig-5vh? [y/n]" → `y` runs the action.

## Files to touch

- `tui/src/App.tsx` — replace stacked right panes with `<DetailTabs>` component
- `tui/src/components/DetailTabs.tsx` (new) — manages tab state, renders LIVE/TRACE/BD/ACTIONS
- `tui/src/components/IssueList.tsx` — full-width rows, stuck detection, status grouping, header counts
- `tui/src/components/Actions.tsx` (new) — actions tab content; shells out via `Bun.spawn`
- `tui/src/state/store.ts` — track selected tab; compute `stuck` per row; group by status
- `tui/src/__tests__/` — snapshot tests per new component, action-keybind tests
- `tui/dist/po-tui` — rebuild via `bun run build` (mandatory final step before declaring done)

## Acceptance

- `po tui` against the current 5+ in-flight runs shows the new layout.
- Selecting a row + pressing `[c]` prompts confirmation; `y` cancels via Prefect API; row updates to `Cancelled` within 2s.
- `[r]` invokes `po retry <id>` and the row appears as new in-flight.
- `[d]` opens a form, accepts issue id + flow + rig/rig-path, dispatches.
- Default tab on row select is LIVE; existing tmux tail behavior preserved.
- Stuck indicator `⚠` appears on the rig-2m2-style 5h-running row in current state; doesn't appear on healthy 4m runs.
- Header counts match issue grouping.
- Done runs collapsed by default; `[D]` expands.

## Out of scope

- TRACE tab content — placeholder only. Filed as separate issue depending on prefect-orchestration-qhg (`po trace` CLI).
- Mobile / narrow-terminal layouts beyond what already exists in `App.tsx`.
- Changing the data model in `store.ts` beyond adding `stuck: boolean` and `selectedTab: enum`.

## Coordination notes

- prefect-orchestration-1qn (RoleTimeline reading task_runs not flow_runs) is upstream of the per-row `step+iter` rendering. If 1qn merges first, lift its store changes; if not, do a minimal `step+iter` derivation here and let 1qn refactor later.
- prefect-orchestration-ld9 (IssueList rows show issue title + readable stage roll-up) overlaps significantly. **This issue supersedes ld9** — close ld9 with a pointer here.
- prefect-orchestration-5vh (FlowOverview header) keeps working — show it above the role timeline inside the LIVE tab.
- Binary rebuild discipline: `cd tui && bun run build` is the **last step** before declaring done. The 5vh fast run shipped source without rebuilding; user only saw the change after a manual rebuild.
close_reason: po simple-mode complete

title: po trace: CLI to inspect agent traces (current + historical) across all role-steps of an issue
description:
**Problem.** Right now to see what an agent actually said/did during a PO run, you have to:

1. `po sessions <issue>` → copy the role's UUID
2. `find ~/.claude/projects/ -name "<uuid>*"` → locate the JSONL
3. `cat | jq` or write a Python script to extract turns / tool calls / token usage

That's three-step archaeology. It blocks both human diagnosis ("why did the planner take 33 turns?") and agent self-improvement (Claude reading its own prior runs to learn). I just did this manually for prefect-orchestration-5vh + oiu trace analysis — the data is gold but inaccessible without scaffolding.

**Goal.** A `po trace` subcommand that surfaces the same data in 1 command, flexibly:

```bash
po trace <issue>                      # all roles, summary table per role-step
po trace <issue> --role builder       # full transcript for one role
po trace <issue> --role linter --tools  # tool-call timeline only
po trace <issue> --tokens             # token + cache breakdown table
po trace <issue> --turn 12            # one specific turn (input + output + tools)
po trace <issue> --slow               # turns > N seconds (find latency outliers)
po trace <issue> --json               # raw structured output for piping to jq
```

**Default output (no flags)** — per-role summary:

```
ROLE       MODEL          TURNS  TOOLS  IN_TOK   OUT_TOK  CACHE_R   THINK  WALL
---------  -------------  -----  -----  -------  -------  --------  -----  -----
planner    claude-opus-4-7   20     13   28,123   19,106  1,133,598    4    97s
builder    claude-opus-4-7   36     23   59,231   12,278  2,437,138    9   121s
linter     claude-sonnet-4-6 17     11   25,402    3,279    918,877    4    40s
tester     claude-sonnet-4-6 16     10   24,109    7,406    898,026    4    37s
```

**With `--tools`** — chronological tool-call list per role:

```
[T+0:02] planner: Bash(bd show prefect-orchestration-5vh)
[T+0:08] planner: Read(/home/.../CLAUDE.md)
[T+0:14] planner: Bash(cat plan.md)              ← REDUNDANT (could be inlined)
[T+0:23] planner: Grep("FlowOverview")
...
```

**Implementation sketch.**

- **Source data**: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<uuid>.jsonl` per role (already exists). PO already records `(seed_id, role) → uuid` in `RoleSessionStore`. Combine the two: get UUIDs from RoleSessionStore, find JSONLs, parse.
- **Project slug resolution**: `~/.claude/projects/<slug>` where `<slug>` is the rig_path with `/` replaced by `-`. Already handled by the Claude Code CLI; can be derived from rig_path the same way.
- **Parser**: read JSONL line by line, accumulate per-turn stats. The schema is documented well enough by inspection (`type`, `message.content[].type` of `thinking|tool_use|text`, `usage` keys). New module `prefect_orchestration/trace.py`.
- **Wire-up**: add a typer subcommand to `cli.py` next to `po sessions` / `po watch` / `po artifacts`.

**Files to touch.**
- `prefect_orchestration/trace.py` (new) — parser + summary helpers
- `prefect_orchestration/cli.py` — `po trace` subcommand
- `prefect_orchestration/cli.py` (also) — link from `po artifacts <id>` output: "for full trace: po trace <id>"
- Tests: `tests/test_trace.py` with a fixture JSONL

**Acceptance.**
- `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh` produces the per-role summary table above (use the actual data — 5vh's JSONLs are in `~/.claude/projects/-home-ryan-24-Desktop-Code-personal-prefect-orchestration/`).
- `po trace prefect-orchestration-5vh --role planner` dumps the planner's full transcript in human-readable form (tool calls + their args + agent's thinking, redacted appropriately for sensitive paths).
- `po trace --json` outputs jq-pipeable data structure with `[{role, turn, timestamp, type, content_preview, tokens}]`.
- Works on **in-flight** runs too (JSONLs are appended live by Claude Code) — current step's trace queryable in real time.
- Works for both `software_dev_fast` and `software_dev_full` (any flow that uses `agent_step`).
- Round-trip-friendly for agents: a Claude Code agent running an analysis task can call `po trace` programmatically, get a digestible summary, propose optimizations.

**Out of scope.**
- LLM-side analysis ("how could this run be faster"). That's a separate `po analyze` later. This issue is just data extraction + presentation.
- Pretty terminal coloring, paging. Plain text output is fine; users can pipe to `less`.
- Surfacing in the TUI (separate issue — TUI redesign that's coming).

**Why this matters.**
Without `po trace`, every "improve the agent" pass requires me (or a human) to reinvent the same JSONL parsing. With it, both humans and self-improving Claude Code workflows have a one-line foothold into the data. This is a force multiplier for the kind of optimization work just done on prefect-orchestration-5vh + oiu.
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: test-unit iter 1 for prefect-orchestration-1uj
description:
You are the **tester** running `unit` tests for issue `prefect-orchestration-1uj` (iter 1). You write missing tests for the new code AND run the existing suite, scoped to the diff. You do NOT alter production code; only test code under `tests/`.

# Layer definitions (non-overlapping)

- **unit** — individual functions / classes in isolation. Mocking external services (HTTP, DB, subprocess) is fine. No real network, no real subprocesses, no Prefect server. Lives at top of `tests/` (or `tests/unit/` when present).
- **e2e** — integration across **real** dependencies. No mocking of the things under integration. Slower (seconds to minutes per test). Lives at `tests/e2e/`.
- **playwright** — browser-driven UI tests. Skip when there's no UI. Lives at `tests/playwright/`.

If you're writing a test that mocks subprocess calls, it's a unit test. If it spawns the real binary or hits a real Prefect server, it's e2e. Don't put both kinds in the same file.

# Read first

```bash
bd show prefect-orchestration-1uj                                     # original issue + ACs
cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-1uj/plan.md                                   # planned tests
cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-1uj/build-iter-1.diff                  # what changed (scope)
[ -f /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-1uj/baseline.txt ] && cat $_                 # baseline test counts
```

# Workflow

## 1. Identify gaps

For each AC the build claims to satisfy, check whether a `unit` test covers it. If not, write one — alongside the existing patterns (don't introduce a new test framework).

Test-design rules:
- Test the user-facing contract, not the implementation. If the code refactors but behaviour stays the same, the test should still pass.
- One assertion per concept (multiple `assert` lines are fine if they're checking different facets of one behaviour).
- Real fixtures over mocked dicts (especially in e2e — see anti-mock list below).
- Cover error paths, not just happy paths.

## 2. Diff-mapped scope (avoid running the entire suite)

The orchestrator computes test scope from the diff. If `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-1uj/tests-changed.txt` exists, use it:

```bash
SCOPE="$(cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-1uj/tests-changed.txt)"
[ -z "$SCOPE" ] && SCOPE="tests/unit/"     # fall back to full layer
```

Otherwise default to `tests/unit/`.

## 3. Run the unit tests

```bash
cd /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration
uv run python -m pytest $SCOPE --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-1uj/unit-iter-1.log
```

(Adapt the runner if the project isn't Python: `bun test`, `make test`, etc.)

## 4. Triage failures

For each failing test:
- **Pre-existing failure** (also fails on `git stash` of build) → not yours; note in log + skip
- **Regression** caused by the build → REJECT (don't fix; that's the builder's job)
- **Failing test you just wrote against the build** → fix the test if it's wrong; otherwise REJECT

# Anti-mock for tests

- Integration / e2e tests that mock the thing they're integrating with → not real tests
- Tests that only verify mocks were called → prove nothing about real behaviour
- Snapshot tests of mock returns → circular
- Fixtures returning hardcoded dicts where real DB/file state belongs

If you find existing tests like this on the diff, flag them in the log (don't fix them as part of this iter — the builder owns that fix).

# Verdict

- **PASSED** — every test in scope passed; no regressions vs baseline; gaps filled with new tests
- **FAILED** — regression(s) detected, OR test gaps you can't fill without altering production code

Append the verdict line to the log, then bead notes:

```bash
bd update prefect-orchestration-1uj.test-unit.iter1 --append-notes "<one-line summary>"
```

# Done — close your bead

Reply with one line: `passed: <N tests>` or `failed: <one-line summary>`.

title: po: control claude model + effort + start_command via flag, env var, and per-role config
description:
**Problem.** The model (`opus`) and the claude CLI invocation (`claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`) are hardcoded in `prefect_orchestration/agent_session.py` and never threaded to `po run`. There's no way to e.g. run `software-dev-fast` on sonnet to cut cost, or run the linter on haiku while the builder runs on opus, or test a CLI flag change without editing source.

**Goal.** Three layered override mechanisms for **both** `model` and `start_command`, with clear precedence:

1. **CLI arg on `po run`**: `--model sonnet`, `--start-command "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"` — flow-wide.
2. **Per-role config file**: `po_formulas/agents/<role>/config.yaml` with optional `model:` and `start_command:` keys — most specific. Lets pack authors set role-appropriate defaults (e.g. `agents/linter/config.yaml: model: haiku`, `agents/builder/config.yaml: model: opus`).
3. **Env vars**: `PO_MODEL=sonnet`, `PO_START_COMMAND="claude --..."` — easiest one-off. Already discoverable via the existing `<rig>/.po-env` mechanism (`_load_rig_env` in `software_dev.py`).

**Precedence** (most specific wins, top→bottom):

```
per-role config.yaml  >  CLI arg  >  env var  >  hardcoded default ("opus" / "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions")
```

Rationale: pack-author declarations of role-appropriate models (e.g. linter=haiku) should beat the operator's `--model sonnet` flag on `po run`. The operator's flag should beat the shell-wide env var. Env var beats nothing-set.

**Files to touch.**

- `prefect_orchestration/cli.py` — add `--model` / `--start-command` typer options on the `run` subcommand. Pass them through to the flow's `os.environ` (so flows that don't take them as kwargs can still pick up via env var) AND as flow-run kwargs where applicable.
- `prefect_orchestration/agent_step.py::_build_session` — load per-role `config.yaml` from `agent_dir`, resolve precedence, pass `model=` and `start_command=` to `AgentSession.run_turn()` (the param already exists at `agent_session.py:168, 268, 341, 627, 1214`).
- `prefect_orchestration/agent_session.py` — already accepts `model` + `start_command`. No signature change required; just confirm they propagate from `_build_session` to `_build_claude_argv` (line 173+).
- **New helper**: `prefect_orchestration/role_config.py` (or fold into `agent_step.py`) — `load_role_config(agent_dir: Path) -> dict[str, str]` that reads `<agent_dir>/config.yaml` if present and returns `{"model": ..., "start_command": ...}` (each optional). Use `tomllib` if you'd rather YAML-free; YAML feels right here since other agent files are markdown.
- **New (optional) sibling files**: `po_formulas/agents/<role>/config.yaml` — none required at this issue (no defaults to ship), but example block in CLAUDE.md / README.

**Acceptance.**

- `po run software-dev-fast --issue-id <id> --rig … --rig-path … --model sonnet` runs every role on sonnet (verifiable in `po sessions <id>` → AgentSession state, and in `tui/dist/po-tui` ROLE TIMELINE which already shows model via `RunTime.model` per `agent_session.py:1683`).
- `PO_MODEL=sonnet po run software-dev-fast …` (no `--model` flag) does the same.
- Adding `po_formulas/agents/linter/config.yaml` with `model: haiku` makes only the linter step run on haiku, even when `--model sonnet` is passed (per-role beats CLI).
- `PO_START_COMMAND="claude --some-other-flag"` overrides the start command everywhere; `--start-command` overrides env; per-role `start_command:` overrides flag.
- Precedence is exercised by a unit test in `tests/test_role_config_precedence.py` (or similar) — fixture an agent_dir with a config, set env, pass CLI arg, assert resolved `(model, start_command)` matches.
- `po run --help` documents the new flags.
- README / CLAUDE.md gets a short "Controlling the model" section (~10 lines) showing all three knobs + the precedence ladder.

**Out of scope.**
- Don't ship any `agents/<role>/config.yaml` defaults in this issue — leave the linter/tester/etc. config files unwritten. We'll opt-in role-by-role in follow-ups.
- Don't auto-detect "this is fast mode, downgrade to sonnet" — `software-dev-fast` defaulting to sonnet is a separate decision (and would belong in the formula, not the session layer).
- No `claude_agent_sdk` swap — keep using the CLI subprocess path. `start_command` only changes the argv prefix, not the transport.

**Reference.**
- Hardcoded defaults: `agent_session.py:168, 259, 268, 341, 593, 627, 1177, 1214`
- argv builder: `agent_session.py:173 _build_claude_argv` — already accepts `model: str` and `start_command: str`
- AgentSession plumbing in agent_step: `agent_step.py:440 _build_session`
- Existing rig-env loader pattern: `software_dev.py::_load_rig_env` (the `--start-command` env var should follow the same `<rig>/.po-env` convention)
notes:
**Scope expansion**: include `--effort` alongside `--model` and `--start-command`. Same plumbing, same precedence ladder.

The Claude CLI supports `--effort <level>` with values `low | medium | high | xhigh | max` (verified via `claude --help`). Threading this through gives operators a third dimension to dial cost vs depth — `software-dev-fast` could default to `effort=medium` (or `low`) for routine work, while `software-dev-full`'s critic / verifier / ralph roles benefit from `effort=high` on the deeper-thinking steps.

**Updated CLI surface** for `po run`:
```
--model       sonnet | opus | haiku | <full-id>
--effort      low | medium | high | xhigh | max
--start-command  "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions"
```

**Updated env vars**: `PO_MODEL`, `PO_EFFORT`, `PO_START_COMMAND`.

**Updated per-role config** (`po_formulas/agents/<role>/config.yaml`):
```yaml
model: opus              # optional
effort: high             # optional
start_command: "..."     # optional
```

**Precedence (unchanged)**: per-role config > CLI arg > env var > default.

**`agent_session.py` change**: `_build_claude_argv` (line 173+) currently appends `--model <m>` to argv. Add a parallel block that appends `--effort <e>` when set. Default = unset (i.e., let the CLI pick its own default rather than forcing a value). New `effort: str | None = None` param threaded the same way `model` already is, through every backend's `__init__` and `run_turn` signatures.

**`AgentSession` state**: stamp `effort` alongside `model` in the per-turn `RunTime` dict (line ~1683) so `po sessions` and the TUI ROLE TIMELINE can surface it. The TUI work for that surfacing can be a follow-up — this issue just wires the data in.

**Acceptance addendum**:
- `po run software-dev-fast … --effort low` runs every role with `--effort low` on the underlying claude CLI invocation.
- `PO_EFFORT=max po run …` (no flag) does the same.
- `agents/verifier/config.yaml` with `effort: max` overrides a `--effort low` flag (per-role beats flag).
- Unit test extended to cover (model, effort, start_command) precedence in one parameterized table.
- README "Controlling the agent" section documents all three dials together.

**Out of scope (still)**: don't pre-set per-role config defaults in this issue; don't auto-couple effort to fast/full mode (formula-level decision for later).

title: test-unit iter 1 for prefect-orchestration-5vh
description:
You are the **tester** running `unit` tests for issue `prefect-orchestration-5vh` (iter 1). You write missing tests for the new code AND run the existing suite, scoped to the diff. You do NOT alter production code; only test code under `tests/`.

# Layer definitions (non-overlapping)

- **unit** — individual functions / classes in isolation. Mocking external services (HTTP, DB, subprocess) is fine. No real network, no real subprocesses, no Prefect server. Lives at top of `tests/` (or `tests/unit/` when present).
- **e2e** — integration across **real** dependencies. No mocking of the things under integration. Slower (seconds to minutes per test). Lives at `tests/e2e/`.
- **playwright** — browser-driven UI tests. Skip when there's no UI. Lives at `tests/playwright/`.

If you're writing a test that mocks subprocess calls, it's a unit test. If it spawns the real binary or hits a real Prefect server, it's e2e. Don't put both kinds in the same file.

# Read first

```bash
bd show prefect-orchestration-5vh                                     # original issue + ACs
cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-5vh/plan.md                                   # planned tests
cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-5vh/build-iter-1.diff                  # what changed (scope)
[ -f /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-5vh/baseline.txt ] && cat $_                 # baseline test counts
```

# Workflow

## 1. Identify gaps

For each AC the build claims to satisfy, check whether a `unit` test covers it. If not, write one — alongside the existing patterns (don't introduce a new test framework).

Test-design rules:
- Test the user-facing contract, not the implementation. If the code refactors but behaviour stays the same, the test should still pass.
- One assertion per concept (multiple `assert` lines are fine if they're checking different facets of one behaviour).
- Real fixtures over mocked dicts (especially in e2e — see anti-mock list below).
- Cover error paths, not just happy paths.

## 2. Diff-mapped scope (avoid running the entire suite)

The orchestrator computes test scope from the diff. If `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-5vh/tests-changed.txt` exists, use it:

```bash
SCOPE="$(cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-5vh/tests-changed.txt)"
[ -z "$SCOPE" ] && SCOPE="tests/unit/"     # fall back to full layer
```

Otherwise default to `tests/unit/`.

## 3. Run the unit tests

```bash
cd /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration
uv run python -m pytest $SCOPE --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-5vh/unit-iter-1.log
```

(Adapt the runner if the project isn't Python: `bun test`, `make test`, etc.)

## 4. Triage failures

For each failing test:
- **Pre-existing failure** (also fails on `git stash` of build) → not yours; note in log + skip
- **Regression** caused by the build → REJECT (don't fix; that's the builder's job)
- **Failing test you just wrote against the build** → fix the test if it's wrong; otherwise REJECT

# Anti-mock for tests

- Integration / e2e tests that mock the thing they're integrating with → not real tests
- Tests that only verify mocks were called → prove nothing about real behaviour
- Snapshot tests of mock returns → circular
- Fixtures returning hardcoded dicts where real DB/file state belongs

If you find existing tests like this on the diff, flag them in the log (don't fix them as part of this iter — the builder owns that fix).

# Verdict

- **PASSED** — every test in scope passed; no regressions vs baseline; gaps filled with new tests
- **FAILED** — regression(s) detected, OR test gaps you can't fill without altering production code

Append the verdict line to the log, then bead notes:

```bash
bd update prefect-orchestration-5vh.test-unit.iter1 --append-notes "<one-line summary>"
```

# Done — close your bead

Reply with one line: `passed: <N tests>` or `failed: <one-line summary>`.

title: verify iter 3 for prefect-orchestration-god
description:
You are the **verifier** auditing whether acceptance criteria are genuinely met for issue `prefect-orchestration-god` (verify iter 3). You verify against a LIVE environment when possible — not just unit tests.

This step prevents "tests pass but feature doesn't work."

# Paths

The bead, venv, and run_dir live under `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration`. Source code for this issue landed in `{{pack_path}}`. When an AC says "installed pack can import X", verify against the **installed distribution** (`cd /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration && uv run python -c 'import <module>'`) — not the source tree under `{{pack_path}}`. Reading source to confirm a change is fine; importability is the consumer-side check that matters.

# Read first

```bash
bd show prefect-orchestration-god                                          # original issue + ACs
cat /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/plan.md                                        # what was planned
ls /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/review-artifacts/                               # smoke / playwright outputs
[ -f /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/baseline.txt ] && cat $_                      # pre-change test state
[ -f /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/final-tests.txt ] && cat $_                   # post-change test state (regression delta)
```

# Verification methods (use the right tool per AC)

## API features
Run `curl` against the deployed (or running) service. Assert specific shape:

```bash
curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/widgets \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"test"}' | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
assert 'id' in d, f'Missing id field. Got: {list(d.keys())}'
assert d['name'] == 'test', f'Wrong name: {d[\"name\"]}'
print(f'PASS: Created widget {d[\"id\"]}')"
```

## UI features
Drive a real browser via Playwright (or via the rig's playwright tests). Assert UI state:
1. Navigate to the feature's URL on the running frontend
2. Perform the user action (click, fill, submit)
3. Assert expected DOM state (elements visible, correct text)
4. Take a screenshot → save to `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/review-artifacts/verify-AC{N}.png`

## Database / data features
Query the actual DB:

```bash
docker compose exec db psql -U postgres -d <db> -c "SELECT count(*) FROM <table>;"
```

## Infrastructure / config
Validate the config file + verify the running service behaves correctly with the new value.

# Anti-mock check

- Production code: any hardcoded sample data, stubbed functions returning fake results, in-memory stores standing in for real persistence, fake auth → these are **not implementations**, they're stubs. If you find one, REJECT.
- Tests: integration tests that mock the thing they're integrating with → not real verification.

# Regression check

```bash
# Compare baseline vs final
echo "Baseline:" && grep -E '(passed|failed|error)' /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/baseline.txt
echo "Current:"  && grep -E '(passed|failed|error)' /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/final-tests.txt
```

If any test that passed in baseline now fails, that's a regression — REJECT (don't punt to the builder; surface explicitly with the failing test ids).

# Confidence rubric

Set a confidence level honestly:
- **HIGH** — every AC verified live (real environment, real curl/playwright/bash), zero regressions, no mock/stub residue
- **MEDIUM** — ACs verified via tests, live smoke partial or unavailable; OR live smoke passed but environment was simulated/stubbed in some way
- **LOW** — couldn't verify some ACs, or environment couldn't be set up

**Refuse to approve at LOW.** Either escalate (`bd human prefect-orchestration-god.verify.iter3 --question="..."`) or REJECT with a reason explaining what couldn't be verified.

title: regression iter 3 for prefect-orchestration-god
description:
You are the **regression gatekeeper** for issue `prefect-orchestration-god` (iter 3). You compare post-change test state against `baseline.txt` and decide if any previously-passing test now fails. You do NOT fix failures; you flag them.

# Where to run

Run pytest in the directory **where the code change lives**:
- If `{{pack_path}}` differs from `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration` (cross-repo self-dev), run in `{{pack_path}}`
- Otherwise (the common case) run in `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration`

# Concurrent-bead safety

Multiple PO flows may run pytest in this rig at the same time. To avoid the cross-bead contention that wedged dgr/etl in 2026-04-29 (per `prefect-orchestration-hyt`), use **per-run cache + per-run output paths**. Don't write to repo-default locations:

```bash
PYTEST_OPTS=(
  -o cache_dir=/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/.pytest_cache    # don't share .pytest_cache
  --junit-xml=/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/junit.xml         # don't share junit output
)
```

If your `pgrep pytest` shows another agent's pytest running, **don't poll-wait** — your `cache_dir` is per-run, so they don't actually conflict at the file level. Just run yours; pytest can have many concurrent processes if their cache dirs differ.

# Run the suite

The orchestrator has already written a path-aware test set to `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/tests-changed.txt` (one test path per line; `#` comments; literal `__FULL__` sentinel = "run the full suite"). Honor it:

```bash
TARGET_DIR="{{pack_path}}"
[ "$TARGET_DIR" = "/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration" ] || cd "$TARGET_DIR"

{
  echo "=== FINAL $(date -Iseconds) ==="
  ARTIFACT="/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/tests-changed.txt"
  if [ -f "$ARTIFACT" ] && ! grep -q '^__FULL__' "$ARTIFACT"; then
    TESTS=$(grep -v '^#' "$ARTIFACT" | grep -v '^[[:space:]]*$' | tr '\n' ' ')
    echo "scoped suite: $TESTS"
    [ -f pyproject.toml ] && uv run python -m pytest "${PYTEST_OPTS[@]}" $TESTS --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -50
  else
    echo "full suite (artifact missing or force-full)"
    [ -f pyproject.toml ] && uv run python -m pytest "${PYTEST_OPTS[@]}" --tb=short 2>&1 | tail -30
  fi
  [ -f package.json ]   && bun test 2>&1 | tail -30 || true
  [ -f Makefile ]       && make test 2>&1 | tail -30 || true
} > /home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/final-tests.txt 2>&1
```

# Verdict

Compare `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/baseline.txt` (pre-change) against `/home/ryan-24/Desktop/Code/personal/prefect-orchestration/.planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-god/final-tests.txt` (post-change):

- **Pre-existing failure** (also failed in baseline) → not yours; ignore
- **New test added by build that fails** → not regression by definition; flag in notes but pass the gate

- **Regression detected**: append the failing test ids to bead notes, close with reason containing `regression: <test ids>`
