Acceptance:
- helm install po ./charts/po works on a real cluster (kind/minikube ok for first pass; EKS/GKE for production-grade)
- Workers register with prefect-server, claim a flow run
- engdocs/work-pools.md updated with the helm path
acceptance_criteria: Helm install works on real cluster; workers connect; doc updated
close_reason: superseded by tyf.N dot-suffix

title: OAuth token refresh persistence in long-running pods
description:
Claude CLI refreshes OAuth tokens and writes them back to ~/.claude/.credentials.json. In a pod that file is ephemeral — refresh writes are lost on restart and the next start uses a stale token from the Secret.

Decide and implement one:
(a) Mount a small PVC at /root/.claude/ so refreshes persist across restarts (simple, requires RWO PVC per pod or pre-pod hashpath).
(b) Sidecar that watches credentials.json mtime, on change writes back to the k8s Secret via service-account RBAC (clean but more moving parts).
(c) Document that pods using OAuth must restart < refresh-token-lifetime, otherwise rotate to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for prod (cheapest, dual-mode policy).

Acceptance:
- One option chosen + implemented + tested across a deliberate pod restart
- Decision recorded in engdocs/work-pools.md (or new engdocs/auth.md)
- Test: pod runs, credentials refresh happens, pod restarts, next claude invocation succeeds without manual intervention
acceptance_criteria: OAuth survives pod restart with chosen strategy; documented; tested
close_reason: superseded by tyf.N dot-suffix

title: OAuth credential injection: CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS env -> /root/.claude/.credentials.json
description:
Bake into the worker image (built by j2p): startup script reads CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS env var, writes it to /root/.claude/.credentials.json with mode 0600, then execs the worker. Pattern from ~/Desktop/Code/agent-experiments/recurring/docker/agent-worker/Dockerfile.

Why: OAuth via subscription is the global rule for non-prod / dev workers; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is the production fallback. Containers should support both — env var presence picks the path.

Acceptance:
- Worker image has /app/start.sh (or entrypoint) that materializes CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS to /root/.claude/.credentials.json before exec
- Falls back to ANTHROPIC_API_KEY auth if CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS unset (use rclaude's ~/.claude.json bootstrap pattern)
- README documents the k8s Secret recipe (kubectl create secret generic claude-oauth --from-file=credentials.json=/home/ryan-24/.claude/.credentials.json) and the env-var alternative
- Local docker-compose path: bind-mount ~/.claude/.credentials.json read-only (no env-var copy needed for local)
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS->credentials.json works in container; API-key fallback works; both documented
close_reason: superseded by tyf.N dot-suffix

title: PO in the cloud: k8s deployment with Claude OAuth, end-to-end
description:
Take PO from laptop-only to a real k8s deployment. Workers run software-dev-full / epic flows in pods, Claude CLI authenticates via OAuth (subscription billing, not API key), bd state is shared, and a cloud smoke test runs an issue end-to-end on a real cluster.

Builds on prefect-orchestration-j2p (base image + work-pool support, already in flight). This epic adds the auth, state-sharing, packaging, and end-to-end-cloud-test pieces around it.

Prior art to lift from:
- ~/Desktop/Code/agent-experiments/recurring/docker/agent-worker/Dockerfile — the OAuth pattern: ship ~/.claude/.credentials.json contents as CLAUDE_CREDENTIALS env var (or k8s Secret), startup script materializes it to /root/.claude/.credentials.json before exec. Stays on subscription, no API key needed.
- ~/Desktop/Code/agent-experiments/recurring/docker/prefect-server/Dockerfile — minimal prefect-server image (FROM prefecthq/prefect:3-latest, EXPOSE 4200, healthcheck on /api/health).
- ~/Desktop/Code/rclaude — Dockerfile with Claude CLI + tmux + non-root coder user; the auth-bootstrap files (~/.claude.json with hasCompletedOnboarding/bypassPermissionsModeAccepted/customApiKeyResponses, ~/.claude/settings.json with bypassPermissions). Useful as fallback / reference; we prefer OAuth path from agent-experiments for prod.

Open risks (decide before shipping):
- OAuth token refresh in headless containers: Claude CLI refreshes tokens and writes back to credentials.json. Pod restarts lose the refreshed token unless persisted (mounted volume, refresh sidecar, or fall back to API key for pods). Decide: (a) persist credentials.json to PVC so refresh writes survive, (b) sidecar that periodically refreshes and updates the Secret, or (c) hybrid — OAuth dev/local, API key prod.
- bd embedded sqlite breaks with >1 worker writing concurrently. Need Dolt server mode (prefect-orchestration-3j8 if that's the bead).
- Rig state in cloud: PVC RWX vs ephemeral git-clone-per-run. PVC for one-rig setups, clone for fan-out. j2p picks PVC for v1; this epic should push the clone path once a git remote exists.

Acceptance:
- A documented, reproducible 'spin up PO in k8s' path (Helm chart or k8s manifests).
- A cloud smoke test that runs one beads issue through software-dev-full on a real cluster (not local minikube/compose) and closes the bead.
- OAuth-refresh story decided + documented.
design: Lift OAuth env-var-to-file pattern from agent-experiments/recurring/docker/agent-worker/Dockerfile. Prefect-server image from same source. Dolt server mode for bd. j2p is a child (base image + work-pool plumbing); this epic adds OAuth, multi-pod bd, Helm packaging, and real-cluster smoke.
acceptance_criteria: Helm chart or k8s manifests deploy prefect-server + worker pool; OAuth via mounted Secret works for at least one full flow; cloud smoke runs one issue end-to-end through software-dev-full on a real cluster; OAuth token refresh strategy documented + tested
close_reason: all 5 children (.1-.5) closed; cloud smoke + helm chart shipped

title: nanocorp-email: replace standalone Automation with deployment-attached trigger
description:
Refactor the email-triage example to use Prefect's `triggers=[...]` argument on `to_deployment` instead of a separate Automation applied via `python -m po_formulas.automations`.

## Why
- Eliminates the apply-step drift problem: today `automations.py` is registered separately from deployments, so editing a trigger in code requires remembering to re-run `python -m po_formulas.automations`.
- Atomic registration: `po deploy --apply` now registers deployment + trigger in one shot.
- Deletes a file (`automations.py`) and a documented step from the README.
- Source-of-truth becomes the pack code, not a server-side Automation that can drift via UI edits.

## Files
- `nanocorp-email/po-formulas/po_formulas/deployments.py` — add `triggers=[DeploymentEventTrigger(...)]` to the `triage_email.to_deployment(...)` call. Trigger expects `nanocorp.email.received`, parameters template `message_id`/`sender`/`subject`/`body` from event payload.
- `nanocorp-email/po-formulas/po_formulas/automations.py` — delete entire file.
- `nanocorp-email/po-formulas/README.md` — remove the `python -m po_formulas.automations` step from the run-it-locally section.
- `nanocorp-email/po-formulas/po_formulas/__init__.py` — update docstring if it references the automation file.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `automations.py` deleted.
- [ ] `po deploy --apply` from the pack dir creates both deployments AND the trigger on the triage deployment in one command.
- [ ] Dropping a fresh email into `/tmp/nanocorp-inbox.jsonl` (with worker running) results in a triage flow run within ~70s, same as before.
- [ ] Verified by: `prefect deployment inspect nanocorp-email-triage/default` shows the trigger on the deployment, and a manual end-to-end test produces a bead.
- [ ] No standalone Automation is created on the server.

## Notes
- Use `from prefect.events.schemas.deployment_triggers import DeploymentEventTrigger` (or whatever the current import path is in prefect 3.6.27).
- Posture: Reactive, threshold=1, within=0.
- Keep `beads_dir` as a default in the deployment's `parameters` dict so it doesn't need to come from the event payload.
acceptance_criteria: automations.py deleted; po deploy --apply registers deployment + trigger in one command; end-to-end email->bead path still works; no standalone Automation on server
close_reason: po-39b6cef0 complete

title: K8s/Docker work-pool support for scaling Claude Code runs
description:
Enable PO to scale out via Prefect's native k8s/docker work pools so software-dev-full and epic flows can run in containers (cloud or local dev).

Scope:
- Build a container image with claude CLI + bd + uv + a configurable formula pack pre-installed
- Document the pool setup: prefect work-pool create po-k8s --type kubernetes (and a docker variant for dev)
- Decide rig-state strategy: shared PVC (RWX, EFS/NFS) vs ephemeral per-run workspace (init-container clone + post-step push). Lean toward ephemeral once a git remote + Dolt server mode for bd are available; PVC as the local-dev shortcut.
- Update po deploy register() examples so packs can opt their deployments into a non-process pool
- Verify TmuxClaudeBackend behavior in a container (likely fall back to ClaudeCliBackend when no TTY); document PO_BACKEND override
- Add a docker-compose dev path that mirrors the k8s shape (worker + Prefect server + a mounted rig)
- po doctor should warn if a pack-declared deployment targets a pool that doesn't exist

Out of scope (separate beads):
- Auth/secret management for claude OAuth token in pods
- Multi-tenant rig isolation
- Cost/billing telemetry

Acceptance:
- A documented path to run po run software-dev-full against a k8s pool end-to-end
- Local docker-compose smoke that runs one issue through software-dev-full in a container
- po doctor surfaces missing pool / image misconfig
design: Use Prefect's native work-pool/worker abstractions instead of inventing a po runtime. Two rig-state options documented; pick ephemeral once git remote + Dolt server are in place. Backend selection (Tmux vs Cli) auto-detected per pod.
acceptance_criteria: Documented k8s + docker work-pool path; local docker-compose smoke runs one issue through software-dev-full in a container; po doctor warns on missing pool
notes:
Prior art: ~/Desktop/Code/rclaude (Dockerfile + entrypoint.sh).

LIFT:
- Base: ubuntu:24.04 + node22 + npm -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code + tmux + git
- Non-root 'coder' user with NOPASSWD sudo (Claude Code refuses --dangerously-skip-permissions as root)
- Auth-bootstrap files written from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY at container start:
  - ~/.claude.json: hasCompletedOnboarding=true, bypassPermissionsModeAccepted=true, customApiKeyResponses.approved=[last-20-chars-of-key], projects.{/workspace}.hasTrustDialogAccepted=true
  - ~/.claude/settings.json: permissions.defaultMode='bypassPermissions', skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt=true
- Run with: claude --dangerously-skip-permissions (already what ClaudeCliBackend does)

AUTH DECISION: workers use ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (k8s Secret), NOT OAuth credentials.json. Aligns with global rule 'production apps / deployed services use API key'. OAuth path is local-dev only.

DROP (rclaude product-specific, not relevant to PO worker):
- ttyd web terminal — PO lurking is 'po watch' / 'po logs' / tmux attach on host
- pixi — we use uv
- entrypoint REPO_URL clone — k8s init-container or PVC mount instead
- Hetzner/cloudflared devenv — Prefect work-pool handles 'where it runs'

ADD on top of rclaude baseline:
- uv + prefect + prefect-orchestration + selected po-formulas pack pre-installed
- bd binary
- entrypoint exec's: prefect worker start --pool $POOL (replacing rclaude's claude-cmd-with-prompt)

Delivery shape: one Dockerfile, per-pack overlay for formula install, docker-compose.yml for local dev, k8s manifest + base-job-template for cloud.
close_reason: po-f84cfcd2 complete

title: Wire mcp-agent-mail file reservations into file-touching prompts
description: Today software-dev-full has no file-reservation primitive. Two concurrent PO flows editing overlapping paths rely on scoped-git-add discipline (prompt guidance) + git merge semantics. No hard isolation. Per principle §5, don't build our own primitive — use mcp-agent-mail which already has file_reservation_paths / renew / release / force_release / macro_file_reservation_cycle + an optional precommit guard. Wire it into every prompt that edits files.
design:
## Prompts to update (po-formulas/po_formulas/prompts/)

- build.md        — builder edits source files and commits. Reserve plan.affected_files on entry; release after commit.
- lint.md         — lint auto-fix may modify files. Reserve the set of files lint touches; release after commit.
- ralph.md        — cleaner refactor pass, occasional edits. Reserve if refactoring; skip if 'no meaningful improvement'.
- docs.md         — writes documentation files. Reserve the doc paths it'll edit.

## Reservation protocol (add to each prompt)

1. At role entry, call mcp-agent-mail file_reservation_paths with paths from plan.affected_files (or the scoped set for lint/docs).
2. If reservation denied (another agent holds paths), call mcp-agent-mail send_message to the holder asking for coordination (CC: {{issue_id}}). Retry after a short back-off, up to N times before failing the step.
3. If a reservation is going to take > default TTL (5 min), call renew_file_reservations periodically.
4. After commit succeeds, call release_file_reservations.
5. On role crash, reservations auto-expire via TTL — no manual cleanup needed.

## Reservation scope

Use issue_id + role as the reservation owner: e.g., 'sr-8yu.3:builder'. Makes collisions legible — 'builder from sr-8yu.3 holds these paths'.

## Optional: precommit guard

mcp-agent-mail ships install_precommit_guard that refuses commits touching unreserved paths. DEFER turning this on globally — it's strong protection but requires every flow to religiously reserve. Document in pack-convention.md as an opt-in escalation.

## Non-goals

- Don't build a wrapper Protocol for reservations — use the MCP tools directly from prompts.
- Don't switch to mcp-agent-mail for agent-to-agent messaging — beads-as-mail is fine for that (principle §5). File reservations are the specific pain mcp-agent-mail solves best.
notes: Principles §5: compose before inventing — mcp-agent-mail already exists. We consume via MCP tool calls from prompts; no new PO code required. Related: worktree-per-run isolation (deferred, separate concern — reservations address the within-rig case, worktrees would eliminate it entirely).
close_reason: Shipped 2026-04-24 in commit 23aca0d: build/lint/ralph/docs prompts now do register_agent + ensure_project then reserve via mcp-agent-mail file_reservation_paths with agent_name='{{issue_id}}-{{role}}'. pack-convention.md documents the convention. AC1-AC5 met; AC6 smoke deferred (would require 2 concurrent flows on same paths); AC7 precommit guard correctly NOT installed by default.

title: po-formulas-retro pack + update-prompts-from-lessons formula
description: First formula pack beyond software-dev. Closes the feedback loop: take lessons-learned.md + decision-log.md content from recent runs, synthesize, propose and commit prompt / skill / CLAUDE.md updates to the target pack's repo. This turns the per-run lessons write-only log into an actual feedback channel. No new PO primitive — reads files, writes files, commits via git. Per engdocs/separation.md §5 item 5 and engdocs/primitives.md §4 (feedback loop as a standing order).
design:
## Pack shape

  po-formulas-retro/
  ├── pyproject.toml           po.formulas + po.deployments entry points
  ├── po_formulas_retro/
  │   ├── flows.py             update_prompts_from_lessons(target_pack, since='7d', ...)
  │   ├── deployments.py       register() returns a weekly cron deployment
  │   ├── analysis.py          lesson-collection + pattern-detection helpers
  │   └── agents/synthesizer/prompt.md
  ├── skills/retro/SKILL.md    how the retro operates, what it's allowed to change
  └── README.md

## Flow steps

1. Locate target pack's install dir (via importlib.metadata.distribution(target_pack).files; trace root). Refuse if not editable (no writable source).
2. Collect lessons: walk <rig_path>/.planning/<formula>/<issue>/lessons-learned.md files for the target pack's formulas, filter by mtime since cutoff.
3. Collect decision-log.md too (reasons agents made non-obvious choices).
4. One Claude turn: synthesize — identify RECURRING patterns only (3+ independent mentions). Produce a proposed diff for prompt / skill / CLAUDE.md files in the target pack.
5. Git handling:
   - Determine current branch in the target pack's repo.
   - If current branch is main/master, create new branch: retro/<utc-timestamp>.
   - Otherwise commit to current branch.
   - Commit with message 'retro(<target_pack>): integrate lessons from <N> runs since <since>'.
   - No push. Humans merge/rebase as they see fit. Git is the rollback mechanism.
6. Write retro-<ts>.md summary artifact into target pack's run_dir documenting what changed and why.

## Recurrence-only bar for integration

Single-occurrence lessons go to 'bd remember' (target_pack: <name>) instead of editing prompts. Avoids prompt bloat from one-off observations.

## Deployment

register() ships one deployment 'retro-weekly' that runs every Sunday; parameters: target_pack. Starter meta-pack registers a deployment per installed formula-pack.
notes: No external dependencies (reads files, uses git). Independent of 4ja.4 (doesn't use overlay). Depends on 4ja.1 (po install) for deployment.
close_reason: Completed: po-formulas-retro pack ships update_prompts_from_lessons formula + retro-weekly deployment + skills/retro/SKILL.md. Fixed test backend signature drift (extra_env kwarg). All 34 pack tests pass; po deploy lists retro-weekly. Confidence: HIGH. Verified: live (po packs install + po list + po deploy) plus full test suite. See .planning/software-dev-full/prefect-orchestration-7vy/verification-report.md.

title: Migrate .beads to dolt-server backend by default
description: Today 'bd init' in a PO rig defaults to embedded-dolt (single-writer). Concurrent 'po run' flows hit 'another process holds the exclusive lock' on every bd update — observed live on parity epic 4ja at 14:15 UTC when 4 child flows tried to claim beads simultaneously. Claims retry through the error but each retry adds latency and occasional drops. Fix: use dolt-server backend by default for PO-managed rigs.
design:
Two parts.

Part 1 — current repo migration (one-time):
  1. Start dolt sql-server on a free port pointing at .beads/dolt (or wherever current embedded-dolt data lives)
  2. Update .beads/metadata.json: dolt_mode=server, dolt_database=<db-name>, add port file
  3. Verify 'bd list' still returns same content
  4. Add a supervisord/systemd unit or 'po doctor' check so the server stays up

Part 2 — make it the PO default for new rigs:
  - 'po doctor' warns on embedded-dolt backend in any PO-managed rig
  - Document 'bd init --backend=dolt-server' in CLAUDE.md as the recommended setup
  - Optional 'po init-rig' verb (future) that scaffolds a PO rig with dolt-server pre-configured, Prefect server URL set, etc.
notes: Blocks effective parallelism of po run epic across many children. Principles §1 + §3 (pack lifecycle — users shouldn't learn dolt config).
close_reason: Verified all 4 ACs already shipped: (1) .beads/metadata.json dolt_mode=server, db=prefect_orchestration; bd list works. (2) CLAUDE.md L34-L44 has '### Backend (dolt-server)' with bd init --server invocation. (3) doctor.py:404 check_beads_dolt_mode + registered in ALL_CHECKS:473; 5/5 unit tests pass; po doctor shows green 'beads dolt mode' row. (4) README.md:170 ## Prerequisites lists dolt with link to CLAUDE.md. Plan iter-2 was verification-only; no code diff this iteration.

title: po.commands entry-point group — pack-shipped utility ops dispatched via 'po <command>'
description: Packs ship small non-orchestrated utility ops (check budget, tail logs, summarize open releases, reconcile CRM, etc.) that aren't formulas and shouldn't pay Prefect overhead. Per principle §4, these are invoked via 'po <command>' (not 'po run <command>'). Discoverable via 'po list' with a column distinguishing 'formula' vs 'command'.
design:
New entry-point group 'po.commands'. Each entry resolves to a Python callable.

  [project.entry-points."po.commands"]
  check-budget = "my_pack.commands:check_budget"
  tail-logs    = "my_pack.commands:tail_logs"

CLI dispatch: 'po <command>' — after checking that <command> is not a core verb (list/show/run/deploy/install/packs/doctor/etc.), look it up in po.commands registry and invoke with --kwargs parsed the same way po run does.

'po list' gains a 'kind' column ('formula' | 'command'). 'po show <name>' works for both.

Collision handling: if a pack registers a command name that matches a core verb, refuse to install with a clear error. Pack authors get feedback at install time, not invocation time.
acceptance_criteria: (1) po.commands entry-point group documented in CLAUDE.md; (2) 'po <command>' dispatches pack-registered callables; (3) 'po list' shows both formulas and commands with 'kind' column; (4) 'po show <command>' prints callable signature + docstring; (5) command name collision with core verbs rejected at install time; (6) example command shipped in po-formulas-software-dev (e.g., 'po summarize-verdicts <issue>').
notes: Principles §1 + §4. Depends on po-pack-lifecycle (08y) so 'po install' is the canonical way to register commands. (superseded prefect-orchestration-z1k)
close_reason: po-dd341cf8 complete

title: Unread-mail auto-inject in AgentSession.prompt()
description: Today builder/critic/reviewer prompts tell agents 'check your inbox first' but nothing programmatically prepends unread mail context for that role. Agents sometimes comply, sometimes don't. Wrap AgentSession.prompt() so every turn starts with any unread mail (from po_formulas.mail) for the current role, framed as a <mail-inbox> context block.
design:
At the top of AgentSession.prompt(prompt, ...), call po_formulas.mail.inbox(role=self.role) (or pass role explicitly). If non-empty, prepend a rendered block:

  <mail-inbox>
  [2026-04-24 12:03 | from=critic] subject: fix build step
  body...
  ---
  [2026-04-24 12:04 | from=verifier] subject: AC5 unmet
  body...
  </mail-inbox>

<prompt text>

After the agent turn completes successfully, mark those messages read (mail.mark_read). Failure: leave unread so next turn sees them again.

Optional flag on AgentSession: skip_mail_inject=False (default False) so stub/dry-run paths can disable cheaply.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Unread mail for the agent's role is prepended to every prompt as an <mail-inbox> XML block; (2) On turn success, those messages transition to read; (3) On turn failure, they remain unread; (4) Empty inbox = no block prepended (no dead tag); (5) Test covers both empty-inbox and with-messages paths; (6) Documented in CLAUDE.md.
notes: Principles §1 + §2 + §4 (orchestrated flow behavior PO owns). (superseded prefect-orchestration-gs5)
close_reason: po-62690841 complete

title: po install / update / uninstall / packs — pack lifecycle CLI
description: Users and agents should only know two CLIs: po and prefect. Today they have to learn uv: 'uv tool install --force --editable <path> --with-editable <other>', and re-run it every time pyproject.toml entry-points change (a real footgun — entry-point metadata is written at install time, not code reload). PO should own pack lifecycle end-to-end.
design:
Four new verbs, implemented as thin shells over uv tool commands. PO introspects installed tool deps and filters for packs (distributions that register at least one po.* entry-point group: po.formulas, po.deployments, po.commands, po.doctor_checks).

  po install <pack>               # from PyPI
  po install <git-url>            # from git
  po install --editable <path>    # for pack authors
  po update <pack>                # re-install so EP metadata refreshes
  po update                       # all packs
  po uninstall <pack>
  po packs                        # table: name, version, source, contributes: <formulas>, <deployments>, <commands>, <checks>

Under the hood: 'uv tool install --force --reinstall <pack>' etc. Error-mapping: if uv not found, print a helpful install pointer (but do not require user to install it manually in happy path).
acceptance_criteria: (1) po install <pack> installs a pack via uv; (2) po update refreshes entry-point metadata for all installed packs; (3) po packs lists installed packs with what each contributes (grouped by entry-point group); (4) po uninstall removes cleanly; (5) README + CLAUDE.md updated — no more 'uv tool install' in user docs; (6) principle §3 cited in PR body.
notes: Principles §1 + §3. Prerequisite for principle §4's commands verb to feel natural. (superseded prefect-orchestration-08y)
close_reason: po-641cde41 complete

title: PO GC-parity rollout — pack lifecycle + mail-inject + agents-folder + extensions
description: Container epic for the 6 GC-parity issues from engdocs/gc-comparison.md §5. Children use dot-suffix IDs so list_epic_children discovers them. Originals gs5/08y/z1k/2rt/99u/0gf are superseded. Dep order: 08y (pack lifecycle) unblocks z1k + 99u; everything else starts in parallel.

'po list' gains a 'kind' column ('formula' | 'command'). 'po show <name>' works for both.

Collision handling: if a pack registers a command name that matches a core verb, refuse to install with a clear error. Pack authors get feedback at install time, not invocation time.
acceptance_criteria: (1) po.commands entry-point group documented in CLAUDE.md; (2) 'po <command>' dispatches pack-registered callables; (3) 'po list' shows both formulas and commands with 'kind' column; (4) 'po show <command>' prints callable signature + docstring; (5) command name collision with core verbs rejected at install time; (6) example command shipped in po-formulas-software-dev (e.g., 'po summarize-verdicts <issue>').
notes: Principles §1 + §4. Depends on po-pack-lifecycle (08y) so 'po install' is the canonical way to register commands.
close_reason: superseded by prefect-orchestration-4ja.5

title: po install / update / uninstall / packs — pack lifecycle CLI
description: Users and agents should only know two CLIs: po and prefect. Today they have to learn uv: 'uv tool install --force --editable <path> --with-editable <other>', and re-run it every time pyproject.toml entry-points change (a real footgun — entry-point metadata is written at install time, not code reload). PO should own pack lifecycle end-to-end.
design:
Four new verbs, implemented as thin shells over uv tool commands. PO introspects installed tool deps and filters for packs (distributions that register at least one po.* entry-point group: po.formulas, po.deployments, po.commands, po.doctor_checks).

  po install <pack>               # from PyPI
  po install <git-url>            # from git
  po install --editable <path>    # for pack authors
  po update <pack>                # re-install so EP metadata refreshes
  po update                       # all packs
  po uninstall <pack>
  po packs                        # table: name, version, source, contributes: <formulas>, <deployments>, <commands>, <checks>

Under the hood: 'uv tool install --force --reinstall <pack>' etc. Error-mapping: if uv not found, print a helpful install pointer (but do not require user to install it manually in happy path).
acceptance_criteria: (1) po install <pack> installs a pack via uv; (2) po update refreshes entry-point metadata for all installed packs; (3) po packs lists installed packs with what each contributes (grouped by entry-point group); (4) po uninstall removes cleanly; (5) README + CLAUDE.md updated — no more 'uv tool install' in user docs; (6) principle §3 cited in PR body.
notes: Principles §1 + §3. Prerequisite for principle §4's commands verb to feel natural.
close_reason: superseded by prefect-orchestration-4ja.1

title: po sessions <issue-id> — list per-role Claude session UUIDs
description: 'po sessions <issue-id>' reads metadata.json from the issue's run dir and prints the per-role Claude 'claude --resume <uuid>' ids (triager, builder, critic, verifier, …) with timestamps and iter counts. Useful for debugging 'why did the builder get confused' — attach to that exact session. '--resume <role>' prints the full 'claude --print --resume <uuid>' command for copy-paste.
design: Pure read-only. Uses bd metadata po.run_dir from 5i9. Pretty-prints metadata.json keys of the form session_<role>=<uuid>.
acceptance_criteria: (1) prints table: role | uuid | last-iter | last-updated; (2) --resume <role> emits a ready-to-run 'claude --print --resume <uuid> --fork-session' one-liner; (3) error if metadata.json missing.
notes: Depends on 5i9. Principles §1+§2. (superseded prefect-orchestration-cdu)
close_reason: po-14057ccd complete

title: po retry <issue-id> — relaunch with a fresh run_dir
description: 'po retry <issue-id>' archives the issue's existing run_dir to '<run_dir>.bak-<ts>' and re-launches 'software-dev-full' on the same issue. Reopens the bead if it was closed. Useful for 'try again from scratch' without manual cleanup.
design: Uses bd metadata po.rig_path + po.run_dir (from 5i9) to know which flow to re-launch and where. If issue is closed, 'bd update --status=open' first, then shell out 'po run software-dev-full --issue-id ... --rig ... --rig-path ...'. --keep-sessions flag to reuse existing metadata.json session UUIDs (default: fresh).
acceptance_criteria: (1) existing run_dir archived with timestamp; (2) issue re-opened if closed; (3) fresh po run launched synchronously; (4) --keep-sessions preserves session UUIDs; (5) refuses to run if another po run for this issue is already in-flight (check via po status / pid file).
notes: Depends on 5i9 (metadata lookup). Principles §1+§2. (superseded prefect-orchestration-qrv)
close_reason: po-37bdadd2 complete

title: po watch <issue-id> — live feed of flow state + run-dir artifacts
description: 'po watch <issue-id>' opens a live view that merges two streams: (a) Prefect flow-state transitions for the issue's active flow run, (b) new files appearing in the run_dir (verdict JSONs, iter diffs, report markdowns). One terminal, both streams, color-tagged by source. Replaces today's 'two terminals + tail -f' workflow.
design: Uses bd metadata po.run_dir (from 5i9) for the filesystem watch (fsnotify or poll). Uses prefect_client for flow-run state polling (subscribes if possible). Merge into a single chronological stream with [prefect] vs [run-dir] prefixes. --replay to dump history before following live.
acceptance_criteria: (1) live stream of both sources in one terminal; (2) Ctrl-C exits cleanly; (3) --replay prints existing artifacts + last N state transitions before following; (4) gracefully degrades if only one source is available (e.g., run finished so no more state changes — still show new artifacts); (5) no extra deps beyond prefect_client + watchdog (optional).
notes: Depends on 5i9 (po logs infra) + 3is3 (po status, for flow-run lookup). Principles §1+§2. (superseded prefect-orchestration-zrk)
close_reason: po-22e5901b complete

title: po artifacts <issue-id> — dump all verdicts + reviews from a run
description: 'po artifacts <issue-id>' resolves the run dir (via bd metadata po.run_dir) and prints a consolidated view: triage.md, plan.md, each critique-iter-N.md, each verification-report-iter-N.md, decision-log.md, lessons-learned.md, all verdicts/*.json. One scroll-through of the full forensic trail. '--open' flag opens the run dir in $EDITOR / file manager.
design: Pure read-only printer. Uses bd metadata po.run_dir from 5i9. Falls back to searching common rig-path locations if metadata missing (warn). Respects less / stdout piping.
acceptance_criteria: (1) prints triage + plan + every critique/verification iter in order; (2) --verdicts flag prints just the JSON verdict files; (3) --open launches EDITOR on the dir; (4) clean output when piped to less.
notes: Depends on 5i9 (po logs) for the bd-metadata lookup helper. Principles §1+§2. (superseded prefect-orchestration-8bd)
close_reason: po-d0ad6948 complete

title: PO verb rollout wave-2 — artifacts/sessions/retry/watch
description: Container epic for the 4 verb issues blocked on 5i9 (now closed). Children use dot-suffix IDs so list_epic_children discovers them. Originals 8bd/cdu/qrv/zrk are being superseded by this epic's children.
close_reason: all children shipped via po runs on 2026-04-25

title: list_epic_children: discover via bd-dep graph, not only ID-prefix
description: Today 'po run epic <epic-id>' discovers children by probing '<epic>.1', '<epic>.2', ... until 3 consecutive misses (gas-city convention). That only works if the user named their children with dot-suffix IDs under the epic. Issues created without that naming (e.g., PO-self-dev verbs with IDs like 'prefect-orchestration-5i9', '-1ij', '-dmy') can't be run as an epic even when they have a proper 'bd dep' graph. Fix: make list_epic_children also walk 'bd dep' parent-child + 'blocks' edges so any connected sub-graph of beads can fan out via Prefect wait_for=. Preserve the current ID-prefix path as a fallback / alternative discovery mode.
design:
Two discovery modes, 'po run epic --discover=ids|deps|both' (default: both):

1. IDs (current): probe <epic>.1, <epic>.2, ... via 'bd show'. Fast, no beads-dep graph required. Kept for gas-city compatibility.

2. Deps (new): run 'bd list --json' once, filter issues where bd-dep 'parent-child' edge points to <epic-id>, OR where any chain of 'blocks' dependencies roots at <epic-id>. Build the sub-graph from those.

'both' (default): union of 1 and 2 with de-dupe.

Also expose an explicit override: 'po run epic --child-ids a,b,c' — bypass discovery entirely, DAG is the 'bd dep' edges between those exact IDs.
notes: Principles §1+§2 (improves the existing po epic verb, no Prefect equivalent). Related: blocks ongoing verb-dev roll-out since we just hit exactly this case with PO self-dev issues.
close_reason: po-a1961cc1 complete

title: po logs <issue-id> — tail or open the run-dir
description: 'po logs <issue-id>' locates the run dir for an issue (via bead metadata 'po.rig_path' + 'po.run_dir' set at flow entry) and tails relevant log files: Prefect flow log, lint/test/e2e logs, decision-log.md. '--follow/-f' streams; default prints last 200 lines of the freshest live/recent file.
design: One-time infra change: at the top of software_dev_full, call 'bd update <issue> --set-metadata po.rig_path=<abs> po.run_dir=<abs>' so any verb can find the run dir by bead lookup. After that, 'po logs' is a shell-out to tail. --merge-prefect flag can come later.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Flow entry sets bead metadata po.rig_path and po.run_dir; (2) 'po logs <issue>' prints tail of the newest log file; (3) '-f/--follow' streams new lines; (4) missing metadata → error with fix hint; (5) no new deps.
notes: Principles §1+§2. Becomes shared infra (po.rig_path in bd metadata) for sessions/artifacts/retry/watch.
close_reason: po-f4e56fb1 complete

title: po doctor — nanocorp wiring health check
description: 'po doctor' checks the full PO wiring in one shot: beads CLI present and reachable, Prefect server reachable (PREFECT_API_URL), at least one work pool exists, every installed formula has a resolvable 'po.formulas' entry point, every 'po.deployments' register() loads without error, 'po list' non-empty, uv tool install is current (no stale entry-point metadata), LOGFIRE_TOKEN env present (warn-only). Exits 0 if all green, 1 if any critical red, with a per-check table.
design: Similar shape to 'bd doctor'. One check per row. Critical vs warn. No side-effects — pure introspection. No new deps; use prefect_client + importlib.metadata + subprocess for 'bd' and 'uv'.
acceptance_criteria: (1) 'po doctor' prints a per-check table; (2) exits 0 when all critical checks pass; (3) red lines include a remediation hint; (4) idempotent, no state written.
notes: Principles §1 (PO-specific wiring) + §2 (CLI-first).
close_reason: po-a8a5fac8 complete

title: po status — runs grouped by bead issue_id
description: 'po status' queries the Prefect server for flow runs and groups them by the 'issue_id' label that PO stamps onto every run. Default view: one row per active or recent issue showing flow name, state, start time, duration, current step. 'prefect flow-run ls' can't do this — it doesn't know about bead issue_ids.
design: Requires software_dev_full and epic flows to tag each run with 'issue_id:<id>' on entry (one-line addition if not present). 'po status' uses prefect_client to list recent flow runs, reads labels, groups. Add filters: --all, --since, --issue-id, --state.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Each flow run is labeled with 'issue_id:<id>' on entry; (2) 'po status' prints one table row per issue with state + timing + current step; (3) exits 0 always (observation, not check); (4) --since accepts relative ('1h') and ISO-8601.
notes: Principles: §1 + §2.
close_reason: po-4d8c86e6 complete

title: Rig-path vs pack-path split — route pack-contrib work to the right directory
description: Today software-dev-full takes a single '--rig-path' argument that represents 'where the beads issue lives and where commits go.' When the beads issue is about developing PO itself (e.g., 'add mail helper to po-formulas'), the rig-path points at prefect-orchestration/ (the core repo), but the work should actually land in software-dev/po-formulas/po_formulas/ (the pack). Observed live on prefect-orchestration-5kj and -shj: both landed their Python code at prefect-orchestration/po_formulas/<file>.py — invisible to the installed pack, only importable if cwd is the rig. Had to hand-move the files into the pack afterward. Need a first-class way to express 'beads issue X is in repo A but the code delta belongs in pack B.'
design:
## Proposals (pick one at implementation time)

(1) Optional '--pack-path' flag alongside '--rig-path':
    po run software-dev-full --issue-id X --rig-path <where-bead-lives> \
        --pack-path <where-code-goes>
    If omitted, pack-path = rig-path (today's behavior). Prompts reference {{pack_path}} as the 'implement here' dir and {{rig_path}} as the 'bd claim/close + bead metadata' dir. Commit happens in pack-path's git repo.

(2) Per-issue bead metadata:
    bd update <id> --set-metadata po.target_pack=/abs/path/to/pack
    Flow reads metadata.json at flow start, uses po.target_pack if present. No CLI change. Easier to bulk-tag an epic's children.

(3) Epic-level config:
    Add '[po]\ntarget_pack = "..."' to the epic's bead metadata; children inherit unless overridden. Closest to how gc rigs work.

Recommend (2) as default and (1) as override. (3) nice-to-have later.

## Prompts to update

- plan.md — clarify 'affected files' live under {{pack_path}}
- build.md — git commit happens in pack_path's repo; the parallel-hygiene section already applies
- lint.md, ralph.md — operate on pack_path tree
- verification.md — 'installed pack can import <module>' check should run against pack_path, not rig_path

## Worktree isolation deferred

Per Ryan 2026-04-24: worktree-per-run isolation is a later software-dev-full version — separate from this. This issue is about CORRECT DESTINATION, not CONCURRENT SAFETY. They compose.
acceptance_criteria:
(1) software_dev_full accepts an optional pack_path kwarg (default: equals rig_path).
(2) Build/lint/ralph/verification prompts reference {{pack_path}} for code ops and {{rig_path}} for bead ops.
(3) bd metadata 'po.target_pack' overrides the CLI default when present on the issue.
(4) Smoke test: run a PO self-dev issue with rig_path=prefect-orchestration and pack_path=software-dev/po-formulas — code lands in the pack, bead updates in core.
(5) README documents the split.
notes: Related: prefect-orchestration-64y, 5kj, shj (the three self-dev runs where this was first observed). Related: worktree-per-run (deferred, separate issue not yet filed).
close_reason: Completed: pack_path split shipped — kwarg in software_dev_full (software_dev.py:668), prompts reference {{pack_path}}/{{rig_path}} (11 agent prompts), bd metadata po.target_pack override via _resolve_pack_path (role_registry.py:148), README §'Rig path vs pack path' (line 105). Smoke-verified by pw4's own run metadata (po.pack_path set). Confidence: HIGH (verified-via-code-inspection).

title: OpenTelemetry spans in AgentSession — Logfire/OTel-compatible observability
description: Every AgentSession.prompt() call is a subprocess ('claude --print --resume <uuid>') that currently leaves no trace in any external observability system. Logfire's Claude Code plugin only captures interactive Claude Code sessions via hooks — it does NOT see our PO subprocess turns. Result: the only record of per-turn agent behavior is on-disk artifacts under $RIG/.planning/software-dev-full/<issue>/. Good for forensics, bad for live monitoring. Add optional OpenTelemetry instrumentation so each prompt() call emits a span, correlating to the enclosing Prefect flow/task run. Logfire is the primary target (Pydantic, already have LOGFIRE_TOKEN in env), but any OTel-compatible backend (Honeycomb, Tempo, Jaeger, Datadog) should work by swapping the exporter — no vendor lock-in.
design:
## Approach

Wrap AgentSession.prompt() in an OTel span. Attach:
- span name: 'agent.prompt' (or 'agent.{role}')
- attributes: role, issue_id, run_dir, session_id, fork_session, turn_index
- events: subprocess.start, subprocess.exit (with exit code, stdout/stderr lengths)
- status: OK on success, ERROR with exception on failure

Propagate trace context from enclosing Prefect task so spans nest under the flow run.

## Provider abstraction

New module: prefect_orchestration/telemetry.py

    class TelemetryBackend(Protocol):
        def span(self, name: str, **attrs) -> ContextManager: ...

    class NoopBackend: ...                  # default, zero-config
    class OtelBackend:                      # generic OTLP exporter
        def __init__(self, endpoint: str, headers: dict): ...
    class LogfireBackend(OtelBackend):     # logfire-configured + pydantic-logfire SDK

Selection:
- env PO_TELEMETRY=logfire|otel|none (default: none if no LOGFIRE_TOKEN present, logfire if set)
- env OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT + OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS for generic OTLP

## Why 'logfire' as a named backend (vs pure OTel)

Logfire's Python SDK auto-configures exporter + auth from LOGFIRE_TOKEN and adds nice Python-specific context (source location, pydantic-model field traces). Pure OTLP works too but needs manual endpoint+header setup. Named 'logfire' gives a one-env-var path.

## Prefect correlation

Prefect 3 integrates with OTel via prefect[otel] extra — it already emits spans for flow/task runs. Our AgentSession spans should set parent context from the current task's span so agent turns nest visually under their enclosing step (e.g. build-iter-1 → agent.prompt(builder) → claude subprocess).

## Non-goals

- Not replacing on-disk artifacts — those stay as the source of truth for forensics and are cheap.
- Not adding a metrics pipeline (counts, latency histograms). Spans only for v1.
- Not instrumenting verdict-file reads/renders — just the LLM subprocess call, which is the cost- and latency-dominant operation.
acceptance_criteria:
(1) New prefect_orchestration/telemetry.py module with TelemetryBackend Protocol + NoopBackend + OtelBackend + LogfireBackend impls.
(2) AgentSession.prompt() wraps each subprocess call in a span named 'agent.prompt' with attributes {role, issue_id, session_id, turn_index, fork_session}.
(3) Span parent is the enclosing Prefect task run when inside a flow — verified by opening a Logfire trace and seeing agent.prompt nested under e.g. build-iter-1.
(4) PO_TELEMETRY env var selects backend: 'logfire' (requires LOGFIRE_TOKEN), 'otel' (requires OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT), 'none' (default, Noop).
(5) Backward compat: no telemetry env → no change in behavior, no new deps imported at runtime. OTel/Logfire deps are optional extras: 'pip install prefect-orchestration[logfire]' or '[otel]'.
(6) Span status ERROR with exception info on non-zero subprocess exit.
(7) Documented in README with a Logfire screenshot and an OTLP example.
(8) A live run of software-dev-full on a test issue shows every step's agent.prompt span in Logfire, with correct role attribute and timing within 50ms of subprocess wall time.
notes:
Dependencies (optional extras, not default):
  [logfire]: logfire>=3.0
  [otel]:    opentelemetry-api, opentelemetry-sdk, opentelemetry-exporter-otlp-proto-http

LOGFIRE_TOKEN already set in env (pylf_v1_us_wNS5gWMRChf1sHwgMxPPyq3bCSKTT43S7Ln9nqVZB5YF).

Related: prefect-orchestration-64y (tmux backend) — tmux output capture is orthogonal to telemetry but both observe the same subprocess; consider if spans should also link to the tmux session name.
close_reason: Completed: optional OTel/Logfire spans for AgentSession.prompt() (commits 13bb3f4, 18b5122). Confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH. All AC verified via tests + in-memory OTel exporter; live Logfire trace (AC #8) deferred to next PO_TELEMETRY=logfire run. No regressions vs baseline.

title: 'po deploy' — register Prefect deployments from formula packs
description: Prefect-native replacement for Gas City orders. Add a 'po deploy' CLI verb that discovers 'register()' entry points in installed formula packs (group 'po.deployments') and calls them — each pack declares its own cron/interval/manual deployments via Prefect's native APIs (flow.serve / flow.deploy). Covers cron, cooldown (interval), and manual triggers. Event triggers use Prefect Automations (UI-configured).
acceptance_criteria: (1) New entry-point group 'po.deployments'; (2) example in po-formulas pack: daily epic-sr-8yu nightly run at 09:00; (3) 'po deploy' lists all registered, 'po deploy --apply' creates them on the Prefect server; (4) README documents the register() convention; (5) existing 'po run' still works unchanged.

title: TmuxClaudeBackend for lurkable agent sessions
description: Third SessionBackend impl that spawns 'claude --print' inside 'tmux new-session -d -s po-{issue}-{role}' so a human can 'tmux attach' mid-run and watch the agent think. Same verdict-file contract as ClaudeCliBackend; output tee'd so orchestrator still reads stdout for session_id/result parsing. Selectable via AgentSession(backend=TmuxClaudeBackend()).
acceptance_criteria: (1) TmuxClaudeBackend in agent_session.py implementing SessionBackend Protocol; (2) named tmux session per (issue,role); (3) 'tmux attach -t po-sr-8yu.3-builder' shows live Claude output; (4) verdict files still land in run_dir/verdicts/; (5) --resume <uuid> works across turns; (6) tmux session exits cleanly (no orphans).
close_reason: po-5a25db7b complete

title: agent_step: per-step timeout budgets instead of one global default
description: Follow-up to prefect-orchestration-hlk. Today landed a single DEFAULT_AGENT_TIMEOUT_S=3600s safety net for ClaudeCliBackend.run. The proper fix is per-role-step budgets so tight steps (triage, learn) catch wedges fast and only legitimately long steps (build, review) get the full hour. Suggested defaults: triage=300s, baseline=600s, plan=900s, plan-critic=600s, build=1800s, lint=300s, test-unit=600s, test-e2e=1200s, review=1200s, regression=900s, ralph=1800s, docs=600s, learn=300s, verification=900s. Wire in agent_step.py: lookup step name -> budget, pass timeout=budget into the backend.run call. Tests: per-step budget table + override via env var (e.g. PO_TIMEOUT_BUILD=N). Without this, any legitimately-long build masks a wedge in a normally-fast step.
acceptance_criteria: agent_step.py looks up per-step timeout from a dict; test asserts each step uses its named budget; env-var override works (PO_TIMEOUT_<STEP>=N); the global DEFAULT_AGENT_TIMEOUT_S becomes the fallback for unknown step names only.

title: po packs: ship a pack-level CLAUDE.md / SKILL so agents discover what's inside without digging
description:
When PO dispatches a software-dev-* agent into a rig, the agent has to discover what packs are installed and what each provides by spelunking through \`pack.toml\`, \`pyproject.toml\` extras, agent folders, and formula source. Result: every fresh agent burns context on discovery before doing the actual work.

## Idea

Each pack should ship a small **operator-facing summary** that any agent can read up front to know roughly what's in the pack — *without* loading every formula prompt or skill body. Progressive disclosure: enough to triage, not enough to bloat context.

## Spec

Pack overlay convention:

\`\`\`
my-pack/
  overlay/
    CLAUDE-<pack-name>.md   # one-pager: what the pack does, what verbs it adds, when to use it
\`\`\`

When a rig installs the pack (\`po install <pack>\`), the overlay file gets symlinked / copied to \`<rig>/.claude/packs/CLAUDE-<pack-name>.md\` (or appended to a generated \`<rig>/.claude/CLAUDE-packs-index.md\`).

Each agent's chassis CLAUDE.md or system prompt includes a \`@CLAUDE-packs-index.md\` line that loads the index — a one-paragraph-per-pack roll-up. Agents read formula / skill bodies on-demand.

## Per-pack overlay shape (~150 words max)

\`\`\`markdown
# <pack-name>

**What it provides:** <one-liner — formulas, agents, skills, CLI commands>

**When to use:**
- <scenario 1>
- <scenario 2>

**Key verbs:** \`<formula-or-cmd-1>\`, \`<formula-or-cmd-2>\`, ...
**Key paths:** \`packs/<pack>/agents/<role>\`, \`packs/<pack>/formulas/<name>.py\`

**Skip if:** <when this pack isn't relevant>

**Read more:** \`packs/<pack>/README.md\`, \`po show <verb>\`
\`\`\`

## Acceptance criteria

- Reference implementation: \`po-nanocorp-starter\` already has \`overlay/CLAUDE-starter.md\` (pre-existing pattern). Generalize the convention.
- \`po install <pack>\` symlinks/copies \`overlay/CLAUDE-<pack-name>.md\` into the rig's \`.claude/packs/\` (or equivalent).
- \`po doctor\` warns when an installed pack has no \`overlay/CLAUDE-*.md\`.
- Update \`po-formulas-software-dev\`, \`po-formulas-retro\`, and the formula packs that ship in starter to include their own overlays.
- Doc the convention in po's README.

## Related

- Existing overlay file: \`po-nanocorp-starter/overlay/CLAUDE-starter.md\`
- Principle: progressive disclosure — agents see roll-up, dig only when relevant.
acceptance_criteria: po install copies pack overlay/CLAUDE-<name>.md into rig .claude/packs/; po doctor warns on missing overlays; reference packs (starter, software-dev, retro) ship overlays; convention documented in po README
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: po: clear stale .retry.lock files (auto-cleanup on resume + po doctor check)
description:
\`po retry\` writes a sentinel \`<run-dir>.retry.lock\` file to prevent concurrent retries. The lock is not cleaned up if the retry process dies before completing — leaving a stale empty file that blocks all future \`po retry\` AND \`po resume\` for that issue.

## Repro (observed 2026-04-30)

- 11:35am: ran \`po retry prefect-orchestration-tvo\` (or \`po resume\` that internally retried) — created \`<run-dir>.retry.lock\` (0 bytes).
- 11:40am: process exited (rate-limit). Lock not cleaned.
- 23:30pm (12h later): \`po resume prefect-orchestration-tvo --at ...\` failed with: \`RetryError: another `po retry` holds <path>.retry.lock; wait for it to finish.\`
- Manual fix: \`rm <path>.retry.lock\`.

## Spec

Two cleanup paths:

1. **On \`po resume\` / \`po retry\` start**: if a \`.retry.lock\` exists, check whether the lock is fresh (mtime within \`PO_RETRY_LOCK_STALE_SECS\`, default 600s) AND whether any live process holds it (best-effort: any pid matching \`po retry|po resume\` for the same issue). If stale, delete the lock with a stderr warning and proceed. If fresh-and-live, behave as today (refuse).
2. **\`po doctor --check=locks\`**: scan \`.planning/software-dev-*/*/\\.retry.lock\` files; report stale ones; offer \`--fix\` to clean.

## Acceptance criteria

- \`po resume\` / \`po retry\` succeed when the only lock holder is a dead process older than 10 min
- A live \`po retry\` is still blocked by its own lock as today
- \`po doctor --check=locks\` lists all stale locks; \`--fix\` removes them
- Warning is logged whenever auto-cleanup fires, naming the cleaned file

## Related

- Watchdog bead (sibling P3) handles the symmetric problem on the Prefect-state side.
acceptance_criteria: po resume/retry auto-deletes stale .retry.lock (>10min mtime AND no live holder process) with a warning; live retry locks still block as today; po doctor --check=locks lists stale locks; po doctor --check=locks --fix cleans them
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: po: worker-liveness watchdog (auto-Fail Prefect runs whose workers died silently)
description:
When a software-dev-* role hits an Anthropic RateLimitError or other unhandled exit, the agent process dies but Prefect doesn't detect it. The flow run sits in \`RUNNING\` indefinitely while \`po status\` claims work is in progress; meanwhile no claude / prefect process exists and no new run-dir artifacts are written. The operator only notices when they check \`tmux ls\` or \`pgrep\` manually.

## Repro (observed 2026-04-30, twice on prefect-orchestration-tvo)

- Run started at 22:30, last run-dir write at 23:05 (unit-iter-1.log).
- At 00:30 (90 min after last write), \`po status\` still showed: \`Running 1h06m test-unit-iter-1\`.
- \`tmux ls | grep tvo\` → nothing. \`pgrep -af "tvo|<run-id>"\` → nothing.
- Manual recovery: \`curl ... set_state {type: FAILED}\` to Prefect, then \`bd update --assignee ""\`, then \`po resume\`.

## Spec

Add a watchdog that runs as part of \`po status\` (cheap, on-demand) and optionally as a periodic Prefect deployment:

1. **Liveness check** — for each \`RUNNING\` flow run with an \`issue_id:*\` tag:
   - Look up the run dir via bd metadata
   - If \`max(mtime over run-dir files)\` is older than \`PO_WATCHDOG_STALE_SECS\` (default 600s = 10min) AND no live process exists for the role's tmux session AND no claude PID matches the run, mark it Failed via Prefect API with message \`Worker silent kill (watchdog)\`.
2. **Bd-claim reset** — also clear \`assignee\` on the bead so a future \`po resume\` doesn't trip the in-progress check.
3. **Surface in \`po status\`** — show stale Running runs with a \`(stale: Nm)\` annotation even before the watchdog auto-Fails them, so operators see suspicion before the cleanup fires.

## Non-goals

- Don't try to distinguish rate-limit from OOM from manual kill — any silent death is fail-and-resume territory.
- Don't auto-resume; that's the operator's call (different rate-limit reset windows, different fix windows).

## Acceptance criteria

- A run dir with no writes for >10 min + no live process is auto-Failed within one \`po status\` invocation
- Bead assignee is cleared atomically with the Failed state transition
- \`po status\` shows \`(stale: Nm)\` for runs with no writes for 5-10 min
- The watchdog never false-positives on legitimately-quiet steps (e.g., long agent thinks); needs to require BOTH file-mtime stale AND no live PID
acceptance_criteria: po status auto-Fails RUNNING flows whose run-dir is silent for >10min AND have no live process; bead assignee cleared; po status shows (stale: Nm) for 5-10min quiet runs; no false-positive on legitimately-active runs
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: po close <id> for clean bd + Prefect manual-closure sync
description: When an operator manually closes a bead because PO state-tracking went wrong (verdict-JSON missing, run_dir damaged, etc.), they currently run bd close <id> --reason='...'. Prefect flow-run state isn't synchronized — it stays Failed/Crashed. po close <id> should: (a) bd close the bead with operator's reason; (b) cancel/mark the corresponding flow-run as Cancelled in Prefect; (c) optionally archive the run_dir if --archive flag passed. Eliminates the cleanup steps when manual recovery is needed.
notes: Pattern hit twice during agent-chassis epic (vsk manual close; 4q1 bd-blocked-while-flow-Completed). Cheap to ship; reduces operator friction during PO-hiccup recovery.

title: po actor-critic: verdict JSON not always written when critique markdown says APPROVED
description: Observed during agent-chassis epic on nanocorps-vsk: critique-iter-2.md ended with 'APPROVED' verdict, but no verdict JSON was written to verdicts/ — so the loop continued to iter-3 and crashed mid-iteration. The work-at-HEAD was correct (pack shipped, commits present); the failure was purely a state-tracking bug. Fix: ensure verdict-JSON write is atomic with the critique markdown's APPROVED conclusion, or have the loop fall back to scanning the markdown for APPROVED/NEEDS_CHANGES/MAJOR_REVISION when verdict JSON is absent.
notes: Single observation during agent-chassis epic (nanocorps-vsk). Manual close was clean. Pattern may recur — file a fix.

title: po resume / po retry: support --time for scheduled re-runs
description:
\`po run\` already supports \`--time\` (relative duration like \`2h\`, \`30m\`, \`+30m\`, or ISO-8601) for scheduling a future deployment of the formula's \`<name>-manual\` Prefect deployment. \`po resume\` and \`po retry\` do not — they fire immediately.

## Why

Two real use cases:

1. **Time-batching for off-peak Anthropic usage.** When Opus weekly quotas are tight during the day, deferring agent work to night (e.g. 7pm or 11pm local) is a real cost optimization. Today the workaround is \`systemd-run --user --on-calendar=...\` shelling out \`po resume\`, which works but lives outside PO's surface.
2. **Recovering from rate-limit failures with cache preservation.** When a flow fails on \`RateLimitError\` after triage/baseline have already produced verdicts, the operator wants to schedule a \`po resume\` for after the reset window — keeping the cached verdicts and only paying again for the failing step onward. \`po run --time\` doesn't help here because fresh \`po run\` doesn't set \`PO_RESUME=1\` and would re-do triage from scratch.

## Spec

Add \`--time\` to \`po resume\` and \`po retry\` with the same semantics as \`po run --time\`:

\`\`\`
po resume <issue-id> --time 4h
po resume <issue-id> --time "2026-04-30 19:00 EDT"
po retry  <issue-id> --time 30m
\`\`\`

- Same parser as \`po run --time\` (relative durations, ISO-8601, \`+30m\` offsets)
- Schedules the formula's \`-manual\` deployment for the future time
- The scheduled deployment must run with \`PO_RESUME=1\` (resume) or with archive-then-fresh-triage (retry) — the deployment payload encodes which mode
- \`po status\` shows the scheduled run as \`Scheduled\` with the fire time

## Implementation notes

- \`po run --time\` already does the work; refactor to a shared helper and call from \`resume\` / \`retry\`
- \`po resume\`'s \`--force\` flag and \`--time\` are orthogonal — both should compose

## Acceptance criteria

- \`po resume <id> --time 4h\` schedules a deployment that fires in 4h with \`PO_RESUME=1\`; on fire, the same step-skip behavior as immediate \`po resume\`
- \`po retry <id> --time 19:00\` schedules a deployment that archives + re-runs from scratch at 19:00 local
- ISO-8601-with-timezone parsing matches \`po run --time\`
- \`po status\` distinguishes Scheduled-resume / Scheduled-retry from Scheduled-fresh-run

## Related

- Workaround used today (2026-04-30): \`systemd-run --user --on-calendar=... po resume ...\` for prefect-orchestration-tvo's 7pm resume after Opus quota reset.
acceptance_criteria: po resume --time and po retry --time accept same time formats as po run --time; scheduled deployment fires with correct env (PO_RESUME=1 for resume, archive-and-fresh for retry); po status shows the scheduled run with fire time
close_reason: Implemented as --at on po resume / po retry (Prefect-native flag name)

title: rig convention: standard make/uv lint+test helpers + document in CLAUDE.md (this rig as exemplar)
description:
**Goal.** Establish a standard for PO rigs: every rig SHOULD provide stable command-line entry points for `lint`, `test-unit`, `test-e2e`, `format`, and document them in its CLAUDE.md. PO's linter/tester agents should prefer these helpers over re-deriving the toolchain (ruff vs eslint vs tsc vs pytest, with-or-without uv, scope flags) inside the agent.

This issue ships the convention for **the prefect-orchestration rig itself** as the exemplar. Other rigs adopt it on their own time per their own bd issues.

**Why** (data from prv trace, 2026-04-29):
The linter ran `bun x tsc --noEmit` 5 times — partly because it had to figure out the toolchain from scratch (looked at `package.json` for "eslint"/"prettier"/tsc), partly because tsc output was large enough to trigger Claude Code's persist-to-disk pattern, then the agent re-cat'd its own outputs. A simple `make lint` (or `bun run lint`) that picks the right tools, captures full output to a file, and prints a small summary would short-circuit all of that.

PO's linter/tester task.md currently lists toolchain detection rules (`pyproject.toml` with `tool.ruff` → `uv run ruff check --fix`, `package.json` with eslint → `bun x eslint --fix`, etc.). That's pack-side. Each rig should provide an opinionated wrapper that PO just calls.

**Convention to ship in this rig.**

Add to `prefect-orchestration` repo:

```
# Makefile (or scripts/po-helpers/)
.PHONY: lint test-unit test-e2e format

lint:
	@uv run ruff check --fix prefect_orchestration/ tests/
	@uv run ruff format prefect_orchestration/ tests/
	@cd tui && bun x tsc --noEmit

test-unit:
	@uv run python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/e2e -q

test-e2e:
	@uv run python -m pytest tests/e2e -q

format:
	@uv run ruff format prefect_orchestration/ tests/
	@cd tui && bun run format
```

Each command:
- Captures full output internally (tee to a log file under `.planning/` or a stable path).
- Prints a small summary to stdout (last 30 lines for failures, "OK" for success).
- Exits non-zero on failure so the agent sees a clear pass/fail.

**Document in `CLAUDE.md`** (this rig's, repo root):

```markdown
## Linting / testing — use the helpers

PO's linter and tester agents (and any human contributor) MUST use:
- `make lint` — runs ruff fix + format on Python; tsc on TS
- `make test-unit` — runs pytest tests/ excluding tests/e2e
- `make test-e2e` — runs tests/e2e (slow; 2-3min)
- `make format` — formatter only

Do NOT invoke ruff / pytest / tsc / eslint directly inside an agent — output may be large and trigger persist-to-disk in Claude Code, leading to re-read cycles.
```

**Coordination with PO core.**

PO's pack-side `agents/linter/task.md` and `agents/tester/task.md` should be updated (separate issue, follow-up) to:
- First check if the rig has `make lint` / `make test-unit`. If yes, use them.
- Fall back to current toolchain-detection logic if not.

This makes the convention OPTIONAL for rigs but ENCOURAGED — old rigs keep working, new rigs get the speed-up.

**Acceptance.**

- `make lint`, `make test-unit`, `make test-e2e`, `make format` exist in the prefect-orchestration repo root and work locally.
- README and CLAUDE.md document them.
- A single `make lint` invocation produces ≤ 30 lines on stdout for the typical post-build state (clean or "X errors, see log at <path>").
- Future PO core change (separate issue) will teach linter/tester agents to prefer them.

**Out of scope.**

- Updating PO core's linter/tester task.md to USE these helpers (separate follow-up issue).
- Adding similar helpers to OTHER rigs (this is the exemplar; other rigs file their own bd issues).

**Why this is the right layer.**

You called this out: helpers like `make lint` belong in the *rig*, not in PO core. PO is supposed to be opinionated about workflow but not about toolchain. The rig knows its toolchain (ruff vs eslint vs both). The agent's job is to call the rig-defined helper, not to re-derive what tool to run. This keeps PO pack-agnostic AND makes each rig responsible for its own cleanup.
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: po tui: TRACE tab — agent-trace scroller per role-step (depends on po trace CLI)
description:
**Goal.** Wire the TRACE tab in the new tabbed right pane (per prefect-orchestration-prv) to the data exposed by the new `po trace` CLI (per prefect-orchestration-qhg). Lets users scroll through a role's full transcript — turns, tool calls, thinking blocks, token usage — without leaving the TUI.

**Depends on**:
- prefect-orchestration-qhg — `po trace --json` data source
- prefect-orchestration-prv — TabbedDetailPane host where this tab lives

**UX**:

```
[LIVE] [TRACE] [BD] [ACTIONS]            ← existing tabs from prv
─────────────────────────────────────────
 ROLE       TURNS  IN_TOK   OUT  CACHE_R   WALL
 planner    20    28K     19K  1.1M     97s    ← ▶ selected
 builder    36    59K     12K  2.4M    121s
 linter     17    25K      3K  919K     40s
 tester     16    24K      7K  898K     37s
─────────────────────────────────────────
 Turn 12 / 20 · planner · 22:06:47
─────────────────────────────────────────
 [thinking 1.2K chars]
   The plan should match the issue's tier (simple)...
─────────────────────────────────────────
 [tool_use] Read
   file_path: /home/.../tui/src/App.tsx
─────────────────────────────────────────
 [tool_result] (1.4K chars)
   import React from "react"; ...
```

Top section: per-role summary table (sourced from `po trace --json <id>`).
Middle: role detail + turn navigator (←/→ to step turns, jump-to-tool with `[T]`).
Bottom: current turn's content (thinking + tool_use + tool_result).

**Filters / keybinds within the tab**:
- `[↑↓]` change role (top section)
- `[←→]` step through turns
- `[/]` filter turns by tool name (e.g., only `Bash`)
- `[T]` jump to next tool call
- `[s]` jump to slow turns (>10s wall)
- `[c]` copy current turn's content to system clipboard

**Live updates**: when the selected role is still in-flight, poll `po trace --json` every 5s and append new turns. Don't lose the user's scroll position on append.

**Implementation**:
- `tui/src/components/TraceTab.tsx` (new) — pane content
- `tui/src/data/trace.ts` (new) — wraps `Bun.spawn(["po", "trace", issueId, "--json"])`, parses, caches per (issue, role) keyed by latest turn timestamp
- `tui/src/state/store.ts` — `traceByIssue: Record<string, TraceData>` slice; refresh trigger
- Tests: snapshot per state (loading, empty, populated, mid-flight)

**Acceptance**:
- Selecting an in-flight or completed run, hitting `[2]` (TRACE tab), shows the role table.
- Selecting a role expands to its turn detail pane.
- Token + cache numbers match `po trace <id>` CLI output.
- Live updates while a role is mid-run.
- No frame drops on a 50-turn transcript (lazy-render, virtualize if needed).

**Out of scope**:
- LLM-side trace summarization or recommendations (separate `po analyze`).
- Cross-issue / cross-flow analytics dashboards (would belong in a `po stats` or web dashboard, not TUI).
notes: Blocked by prefect-orchestration-qhg (po trace CLI) and prefect-orchestration-prv (TabbedDetailPane). Don't dispatch until both close.

title: po tui: FlowOverview shows 'graph_run' on epic children — add graph_run to registry OR resolve leaf flow name
description:
**Problem.** When a row is an epic-fanned-out child (e.g. `rig-4lp` dispatched via `epic` → `graph_run` → `software_dev_full` subflow), the FlowOverview header in the right pane shows `graph_run` with no overview blurb, instead of `software_dev_full` with its full stage list.

Repro:
- `po run epic --epic-id <id> ...` fans children out via `graph_run`
- Each child runs `software_dev_full` as a subflow
- Open `po tui`, select a child row
- Expected: header shows `software_dev_full · triage → plan ⟲ → build → lint+test → ...`
- Actual: header shows `graph_run` (no overview because graph_run isn't in `FLOW_OVERVIEWS`)

**Diagnosis.** In `tui/src/state/store.ts` (the `flowEntries.map(...)` block around line ~377), `flowName: flowNames[fr.flow_id]` resolves `flow_id` to the deployment-level flow function name. For epic children, `fr.flow_id` points at `graph_run` rather than the `software_dev_full` subflow — Prefect's `parent_task_run_id` linkage already exists in the row (`parentFlowRunId`), but the row's own `flow_id` is the parent's, not its own.

**Two fixes — pick whichever the planner finds simpler:**

**Option A (1-line, ship today)**: add `graph_run` to `FLOW_OVERVIEWS` in `tui/src/components/FlowOverview.tsx` with a blurb like:
```
graph_run: "graph fan-out: parallel children dispatched as subflows"
```

This handles the rendering gap immediately, even if the row's `flowName` is technically the parent. Children + parent rows both look reasonable.

**Option B (correct fix, more work)**: in `store.ts`, resolve a row's `flowName` by walking down to the innermost subflow that the row represents — not the deployment-level parent. Specifically: detect when a flow_run is hosting subflows, and either (a) render the subflow as its own IssueRow (so each subflow shows its own header), or (b) when this isn't already happening, add a `subflowName` field that overrides `flowName` for display. The TUI's `parentFlowRunId` linkage already exists; this is the inverse direction (parent → leaf).

**Recommendation: ship A, file a follow-up for B.** A is a one-line PR; B needs a proper data-shape conversation about how subflows surface in the IssueRow hierarchy and is closer to the scope of `prefect-orchestration-1qn` (RoleTimeline reading task_runs vs flow_runs).

**Files (Option A path).**
- `tui/src/components/FlowOverview.tsx` — add the entry to `FLOW_OVERVIEWS`. Update existing test at `tui/src/__tests__/flowOverview.test.ts` to cover `graph_run`.
- `tui/dist/po-tui` — rebuild via `cd tui && bun run build`.

**Acceptance.**
- `po tui` opened against an in-flight epic run (e.g. `uc0-e2e` rig with `rig-4lp` child) shows `graph_run · graph fan-out: ...` blurb, not bare `graph_run`.
- Existing `software_dev_full` and `epic` cases still render their blurbs.
- Test passes.
- Binary rebuilt — verify with `stat -c "%y" tui/dist/po-tui` post-commit ≥ source change time.

**Note on rebuild discipline.** The 5vh fast run shipped FlowOverview source but did NOT rebuild the binary; user only saw it after a manual rebuild. For this issue the agent MUST run `bun run build` as the final step before declaring done — otherwise the change is invisible to the user.
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: po tui: flow-overview header above bd-show pane (what does this flow run?)
description:
**Problem.** The bd-show pane (shipped in `a3600b9`, `tui/src/components/BdShow.tsx`) shows the issue title + body, but there's no per-flow context describing what \"software_dev_full\" actually does (or what `epic` does). A reader pulling the TUI up cold has to remember the flow's role sequence from CLAUDE.md.

**Goal.** Above the bd-show pane (or as a thin header bar inside the right column), render a 1-2 line flow overview when a row is selected:

- For `software_dev_full`: \"triage → plan ⟲ → build → lint+test → regression → review ⟲ → deploy-smoke → verification ⟲ → ralph ⟲ → docs → learn\"
- For `epic`: \"epic fan-out: DAG-ordered parallel children\"
- Fallback: just the flow name.

The text is short and static per flow. Source it from a small map keyed off `flow_name` (or whatever field already drives `IssueRow.flowName` / `flowStateName`).

**Files.**
- `tui/src/components/RoleTimeline.tsx` or a new `FlowOverview.tsx` sibling
- Wire-up in whichever parent renders the right column (search for `<RoleTimeline` and `<BdShow`)

**Acceptance.**
- Selecting a `software_dev_full` row shows the stage list in the right column.
- Selecting an `epic` row shows the epic line.
- No layout regressions; bd-show body still readable.
- One unit/snapshot test per flow string.

**Out of scope.** Dynamic stage list per flow registry — hardcoding the two flow strings is fine for this issue. If we add more flows later, factor into a registry then.
close_reason: po fast-mode complete

title: Surface complexity tier in software_dev_full flow result
description:
Today `software_dev_full` returns the dict { status, mode, complexity, plan_iter, build_iter, ... } only on the full-pipeline path. The docs_only short path returns { status: 'docs_only_completed', mode: 'simple', complexity } — but the trivial path returns { status: 'trivial_completed', mode: 'simple', complexity: 'trivial' } without plan_iter/build_iter (correctly None for trivial).

Add a single `logger.info` line at the very top of the flow body (right after _load_rig_env) that logs the chosen complexity tier prominently, separate from the existing 'triage:' line that runs after triage. The very-first-line log helps when reading flow logs in po watch — currently you have to scroll past the triage step to see what tier was picked.

Acceptance:
- 'po watch' / Prefect logs show 'complexity tier: <tier>' as one of the first log lines (right after the rig path is resolved)
- All 35 existing pack tests continue to pass
- One new unit test asserts the log message format

This is moderate complexity: the change is one logger.info line + one test, but it touches the flow body so should run through plan + build + lint + unit-test + review.
acceptance_criteria: logger.info('complexity tier: %s', tier) added near top of flow body; one new test in tests/test_software_dev_*.py asserts the log line; all 35+ pack tests pass
close_reason: po simple-mode complete

title: nanoc: Honcho dialectic user-modeling for the board-of-one
description:
Add a persistent user-model layer for the human board-of-one across every nanocorp's agents, using plastic-labs/honcho (same library hermes-agent integrates).

Why: a board-of-one delegates to many corps and many agent roles, but every one of them is predicting the *same* person — risk tolerance, decision style, what gets reserved vs. delegated, communication preferences. Today this lives implicitly in conversation context and `bd remember` notes per repo. Honcho gives us a dialectic user model that updates from every conversation and can be queried before any agent turn ("what would Ryan likely decide here?").

Distinct from existing memory:
- bd remember / auto-memory = facts about projects, code, prior incidents.
- Honcho = behavioural model of the *user* (preferences, style, history of choices).

Both are useful; this is the missing half.
design:
- Honcho client lives in nanoc, not in po-formulas — it's a nanoc-level concern (the human is per-installation, not per-corp).
- Prompt assembly: `nanoc talk` appends a `<board-of-one-profile>` block from Honcho before the agent system prompt. Block size capped (~500 tokens) to avoid blowing up cheap turns.
- Feedback loop: post-turn hook ships the (user-msg, agent-msg) pair to Honcho async; failure is non-fatal.
- Couple loosely with the messaging gateway issue: gateway turns also feed Honcho, so the model learns from Telegram convos, not only laptop ones.
acceptance_criteria:
- Honcho deployed (self-hosted or hosted) and reachable from nanoc.
- nanoc talk injects a board-of-one user-model snippet into the assembled prompt for every agent (configurable, can be disabled per agent).
- Each conversation's turns get fed back to Honcho so the model improves.
- A `nanoc me` command prints the current user model for inspection / correction.
- Works across all registered nanocorps using a single shared user (one human, many corps).
notes: Reference: plastic-labs/honcho (the lib hermes-agent uses). Companion to the messaging-gateway bead.
close_reason: moved to nanocorps/beads root: beads-otj (gateway), beads-1jf (honcho)

title: RemoteComputer backend abstraction (persistent=bool) for spawning workers on their own machines
description:
Add a RemoteComputer abstraction so PO can spawn agent workers on machines other than the orchestrator host — local container, SSH host, Daytona sandbox, Modal sandbox, K8s pod — with an opt-in persistence flag that hibernates the worker's filesystem/process between turns.

## Motivation

PO's current SessionBackend Protocol (agent_session.py: ClaudeCliBackend, TmuxClaudeBackend, StubBackend) all run the Claude CLI as a child process of the Prefect worker on the orchestrator host. That couples worker concurrency to orchestrator host resources and gives no story for:

- isolating one rig's filesystem from another (parallel epics in the same dir step on each other today — see prefect-orchestration-nfs lessons)
- running workers on cheap remote machines (a $5 VPS, a GPU box) while orchestration stays local
- pausing a worker between turns without paying for an idle VM (Modal/Daytona hibernation)

Nous's hermes-agent solves the same shape via tools/environments/{base.py, local.py, docker.py, ssh.py, daytona.py, modal.py, singularity.py}: a BaseEnvironment ABC with cleanup/init_session/execute, plus per-backend persistence (Daytona persistent_filesystem=True resumes sandboxes; Modal stores per-task filesystem snapshots in modal_snapshots.json and restores on next spawn).

## Proposed shape

New Protocol in prefect_orchestration/remote_computer.py:

    class RemoteComputer(Protocol):
        backend: str                  # "local" | "docker" | "ssh" | "daytona" | "modal"
        persistent: bool              # if True, hibernate between turns and resume on next prompt
        workspace: str                # cwd inside the computer
        def start(self, *, task_id: str) -> None: ...
        def execute(self, argv: list[str], *, env: dict[str,str], stdin: str | None) -> CompletedProcess: ...
        def upload(self, local: Path, remote: str) -> None: ...
        def download(self, remote: str, local: Path) -> None: ...
        def hibernate(self) -> None: ...   # noop if persistent=False
        def cleanup(self) -> None: ...

SessionBackend (existing) gets a new ctor field:

    @dataclass
    class ClaudeCliBackend:
        computer: RemoteComputer | None = None   # None = run locally as today

When set, prompt() builds the claude argv as today but dispatches via computer.execute(...) instead of subprocess.Popen. Tmux backend layers on top — tmux session lives inside the RemoteComputer. The rig path mounts in via upload/download or a bind mount declared at start().

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] RemoteComputer Protocol + LocalComputer (default, equivalent to today) land in core
- [ ] ClaudeCliBackend gains optional `computer=` kwarg; existing tests pass unchanged with computer=None
- [ ] One additional backend lands as proof — pick the cheapest (DockerComputer locally, since Dockerfile already exists in repo)
- [ ] persistent=True path on the proof backend: spawn → run a turn that writes /tmp/x → hibernate → cleanup → re-start with same task_id → /tmp/x still readable
- [ ] po doctor gains `check_remote_computer_backend` per active backend (modal CLI present? daytona token set? docker reachable?)
- [ ] CLI: `po run software-dev-full --computer docker --persistent` plumbs through to the formula's backend selection

## Out of scope (later beads)

- Daytona / Modal / SSH / Singularity backends (one bead each — model after hermes's 250-460 LoC modules)
- Multi-computer fan-out (each role on its own computer) — current scope is one-computer-per-flow
- Worker pool (a pool of warm hibernated computers) — depends on this landing first

## References

- hermes-agent: tools/environments/base.py:267 (BaseEnvironment ABC)
- hermes-agent: tools/environments/daytona.py:30 (persistent_filesystem flag)
- hermes-agent: tools/environments/modal.py:34 (snapshot store + restore)
- po: prefect_orchestration/agent_session.py (SessionBackend Protocol — extension point)
